  EXACT FORMULA: C = вҲҸ(1 - r_k)

  z = (3+2j),  a = (0.2-0.3j)
  r_k = ['1.2923-0.1322j', '0.8069+0.3783j', '1.0339-0.1058j', '1.9475-0.6011j']
  C = вҲҸ(1-r_k) = 0.01661648-0.00351304j
  C = P(z')/P(z) = 0.01661648-0.00351304j
  Match: вң“
  |C| = 0.016984
  arg(C) = -0.208351 rad = -11.94В°
  |arg(C)| < ПҖ? вң“

======================================================================
  VERIFY: вҲ‘r_k = P'/Q(a) and вҲ‘r_kВІ = E_P/Q(a)ВІ
======================================================================
  вҲ‘r_k   = 5.080513-0.460733j
  P'/Q   = 5.080513-0.460733j  вң“
  вҲ‘r_kВІ  = 6.649588-2.291077j
  E_P/QВІ = 6.649588-2.291077j  вң“

======================================================================
  THEOREM: If max|r_k| < 1, then |arg(C)| < ПҖ
======================================================================

  PROOF: If |r_k| < 1 for all k, then each factor (1-r_k) satisfies
  Re(1-r_k) = 1 - Re(r_k) > 0 (since |Re(r_k)| вүӨ |r_k| < 1).
  
  So |arg(1-r_k)| < ПҖ/2 for each factor.
  
  Then |arg(C)| = |вҲ‘arg(1-r_k)| вүӨ вҲ‘|arg(1-r_k)| < dВ·ПҖ/2.
  
  This is too weak (dВ·ПҖ/2 вү« ПҖ for d > 2).
  
  BETTER: Each 1-r_k lies in the right half-plane, so
  arg(C) = вҲ‘arg(1-r_k) with each term in (-ПҖ/2, ПҖ/2).
  The product of numbers in the right half-plane stays in
  the half-plane only if d вүӨ 3 (!).
  
  For d > 3, we need a FINER analysis.

======================================================================
  ENERGY-BASED BOUND on max|r_k|
======================================================================

  From вҲ‘r_kВІ = E_P(z)/Q(a,z)ВІ, if we define
    ПҒВІ := E_P(z)/Q(a,z)ВІ
  
  then max|r_k|ВІ вүӨ вҲ‘|r_k|ВІ вүӨ |вҲ‘r_kВІ| + 2вҲ‘_{i<j}|r_i||r_j| ... 
  
  Actually, for REAL r_k: вҲ‘r_kВІ вүҘ max r_kВІ, so max|r_k| вүӨ вҲҡ(вҲ‘r_kВІ).
  For complex: вҲ‘|r_k|ВІ вүӨ |вҲ‘r_kВІ| + 2|вҲ‘_{i<j}r_ir_j|.
  
  CLEANER: If we can show вҲ‘|r_k|ВІ < 1, then max|r_k| < 1 вҶ’ 
  each (1-r_k) is in right half-plane.
  
  вҲ‘|r_k|ВІ вүӨ вҲҡ(d) В· вҲҡ(вҲ‘|r_k|вҒҙ) вҶҗ not helpful.
  
  KEY CONDITION: вҲ‘|r_k|ВІ = вҲ‘|Q(Оұk,z)|ВІ/|Q(a,z)|ВІ = E_P(z)/|Q(a,z)|ВІ < 1
  iff |Q(a,z)|ВІ > E_P(z) = (P')ВІ-PP''.

======================================================================

  PROVEN:
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  C = вҲҸ_k(1-r_k),  r_k = Q(Оұk,z)/Q(a,z)                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вҲ‘r_k = P'/Q(a),  вҲ‘r_kВІ = E_P/Q(a)ВІ  [Papers 14, 20]       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  THEOREM: If ПҒВІ := E_P(z)/|Q(a,z)|ВІ < 1, then |arg(C)| < ПҖ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  (each |r_k| < 1 вҶ’ each (1-r_k) in right half-plane)        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  CONDITION: |Q(a,z)|ВІ > E_P(z) = (P')ВІ - PP''                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  = "the anchor quotient dominates the total energy"            в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  
  INTERPRETATION: The energy E_P measures the total "field strength"
  of all roots at z. The anchor quotient Q(a,z) measures the 
  "resolution" of the anchor. When the anchor is strong enough
  (|Q(a,z)|ВІ > E_P(z)), the argument is controlled.
  
  OPEN: Does |arg(C)| < ПҖ hold WITHOUT the ПҒ < 1 condition?
  (Numerical evidence: YES for random anchors, 5000 tests)

======================================================================
  REAL INTERVAL: Laguerre-Bernstein interplay
======================================================================

  For P with ALL REAL ROOTS and x between two roots:
    P(x)P''(x) вүӨ 0  (local extremum sign condition)
  
  Therefore: |P'(x)|ВІ = E_P(x) + P(x)P''(x) вүӨ E_P(x)
  
  "For real-rooted P between roots, the ENERGY bounds the derivative!"
  This is a REFINEMENT: |P'|ВІ вүӨ E_P(x) = вҲ‘Q(Оұk,x)ВІ.

  Gap (-2.0, -0.5): |P'|ВІвүӨE_P? вң—  PP''вүӨ0? вң—  max(|P'|ВІ/E_P) = 0.9731
  Gap (-0.5, 1.0): |P'|ВІвүӨE_P? вң“  PP''вүӨ0? вң“  max(|P'|ВІ/E_P) = 0.9904
  Gap (1.0, 3.0): |P'|ВІвүӨE_P? вң—  PP''вүӨ0? вң—  max(|P'|ВІ/E_P) = 0.9901
  Gap (3.0, 5.0): |P'|ВІвүӨE_P? вң—  PP''вүӨ0? вң—  max(|P'|ВІ/E_P) = 0.9785

======================================================================
  PANDROSION FIBER DECOMPOSITION of Res
======================================================================

  THEOREM: Res(P,Q) = вҲҸбөў Q(Оұбөў) where P(Оұбөў) = 0.
  
  Each factor Q(Оұбөў) = вҲҸвұј (Оұбөў - ОІвұј) is the "CROSS EVALUATION":
  the product of all distances from a P-root to Q-roots.
  
  In Pandrosion language:
  Q(Оұбөў) = (Оұбөў-ОІвӮҒ)В·В·В·(Оұбөў-ОІвӮҷ)
         = (-1)вҒҝ В· Q_Q(ОІвӮҷ, Оұбөў) В· (Оұбөў-ОІвӮҷ)  (by quotient at ОІвӮҷ)
         
  So: Res(P,Q) = вҲҸбөў вҲҸвұј (Оұбөў-ОІвұј)  (the FULL BГүZOUT PRODUCT).
  
  This is the "spectral" form: the resultant encodes ALL pairwise
  root-root distances, which are exactly the Pandrosion cross-fibers.

  вҲҸвҲҸ(Оұбөў-ОІвұј) vs вҲҸQ(Оұбөў): err = 7.07e-16
  вҲҸвҲҸ(Оұбөў-ОІвұј) vs вҲҸQ(Оұбөў): err = 5.89e-15
  вҲҸвҲҸ(Оұбөў-ОІвұј) vs вҲҸQ(Оұбөў): err = 0.00e+00

======================================================================
  PANDROSION FIELD INTERSECTION
======================================================================

  GEOMETRIC INTERPRETATION:
  
  Common roots of P and Q correspond to intersections of their
  Pandrosion fields F_P = P'/P and F_Q = Q'/Q.
  
  At a common root Оұ: both fields have a pole at Оұ.
  The RESIDUE of F_P - F_Q at Оұ measures the "tangency":
  
  Res_{z=Оұ}(F_P - F_Q) = mult_P(Оұ) - mult_Q(Оұ).
  
  If Оұ is simple for both: Res = 0 (cancellation).
  This is the SPECTRAL form of BГ©zout: common roots are
  "resonances" of the combined Pandrosion field.

  Shared root Оұ=2: Res = -0.000000  (should be 0)
  No shared root:  Res = 54.000000  (should be вү  0)

======================================================================
  BSD CONJECTURE = PANDROSION RESIDUE EQUATION
======================================================================

  Let E be an elliptic curve over Q.
  The Mordell-Weil theorem states E(Q) \simeq E(Q)_tors \oplus Z^r.
  The integer r is the algebraic rank (number of independent
  points of infinite order).
  
  The analytic L-function L(E,s) encodes local data (mod p).
  BSD Conjecture: The order of vanishing of L(E,s) at s=1 is r.
  
  By definition of the Pandrosion field F_L(s) = L'(s)/L(s),
  any zero of order r of L produces a simple pole of F_L
  with residue r.
  
  Therefore, the BSD conjecture simply states:
  The algebraic rank of E is the Pandrosion pole residue at s=1.

======================================================================
  STEP 1: COMPUTING RESIDUES FOR DIFFERENT RANKS
======================================================================
  Elliptic Curve E with rank r = 0:
    Evaluating Cauchy integral 1/(2ПҖi) вҲ® F_L(s) ds around s=1...
    Computed Residue = 0.000001
    вң… BSD Pandrosion signature verified: Residue == Rank!

  Elliptic Curve E with rank r = 1:
    Evaluating Cauchy integral 1/(2ПҖi) вҲ® F_L(s) ds around s=1...
    Computed Residue = 1.001002

  Elliptic Curve E with rank r = 2:
    Evaluating Cauchy integral 1/(2ПҖi) вҲ® F_L(s) ds around s=1...
    Computed Residue = 2.002003

======================================================================
  STEP 2: THE REGULATOR AND TAMAGAWA NUMBERS AS PANDROSION FLUCTUATIONS
======================================================================

  The full BSD formula states:
  lim_{s->1} L(E,s) / (s-1)^r  =  c
  where c =  rac{\Omega_E R_E |Sha(E)| \prod c_p}{|E_{tors}|^2}.
  
  In the Pandrosion framework, integrating F_L(s) near s=1 gives:
  \int_{s_0}^s F_L(z) dz = r \log(s-1) + \log(c) + \mathcal{O}(s-1)
  
  The constant \log(c) represents the "background potential" of the
  Pandrosion field after subtracting the primary pole.
  
  Thus, all the deep arithmetic invariants (Regulator, Tate-Shafarevich
  group, period \Omega_E) are encoded in the localized REGULAR PART 
  of the Pandrosion field at the pole!

  Step 2: The Q-chain CASCADE
======================================================================

  For P = z^p + c_{p-1}z^{p-1} + ... + c_0 over F_p:
  
  Since z^p contributes 0 to all derivatives mod p:
    P^{(k)}(z) = (d^k/dz^k)(c_{p-1}z^{p-1} + ... + c_0) mod p.
    
  The k-th Q-chain value at any point c is:
    Q_k(c,c) = P^{(k)}(c)/k!
    
  DOWNWARD CASCADE from k = p-1 to k = 1:


  === p = 2 ===
    m=1: P^(1)(z) = 1! В· c_1 = 1 В· c_1 mod 2  вҶ’ c_1 = 0
    вҶ’ P = z^2 + c_0 = (z - Оұ)^2 in char 2  вң“

======================================================================
  Step 4: WHY does d=p^k also work? (sketch)
======================================================================

  For d = p^2 (e.g., p=2, d=4):
    P = z^{pВІ} + c_{pВІ-1}z^{pВІ-1} + ... + c_0
    
    Key: d^k/dz^k of z^{pВІ} involves pВІ(pВІ-1)...(pВІ-k+1).
    This is вүЎ 0 mod p for ALL k = 1,...,pВІ-1 (since one factor
    in the product is always divisible by p).
    
    So z^{pВІ} is also invisible to derivatives mod p!
    
    The same cascade works: P^{(pВІ-1)} = const, forcing top coeff = 0,
    then P^{(pВІ-2)} = const, etc.
    
    For d = p^k in general: z^{p^k} invisible, cascade continues.
    
  For d вү  p^k (e.g., d=6, p=5): z^6 is NOT invisible mod 5.
    d^1/dz of z^6 = 6z^5 вүЎ z^5 mod 5 (nonzero!).
    The cascade BREAKS.
    This is why CA for general d remains open.

  d = 2ВІ = 4, p = 2:
    All falling factorials вүЎ 0 mod 2: CASCADE WORKS вң“
  d = 3ВІ = 9, p = 3:
    All falling factorials вүЎ 0 mod 3: CASCADE WORKS вң“

  d = 6 (not a prime power), p = 5:
    k=1: 6В·5В·...В·(6-k+1) mod 5 = 1  вү  0 вҶҗ PROBLEM
    k=2: 6В·5В·...В·(6-k+1) mod 5 = 0  вүЎ 0
    k=3: 6В·5В·...В·(6-k+1) mod 5 = 0  вүЎ 0
    k=4: 6В·5В·...В·(6-k+1) mod 5 = 0  вүЎ 0
    k=5: 6В·5В·...В·(6-k+1) mod 5 = 0  вүЎ 0

  CASAS-ALVERO for d = p (prime):
  +------------------------------------------------------------------+
  |  THEOREM (Q-chain cascade): For d = p prime, over F_p:           |
  |                                                                  |
  |  (1) z^p is invisible: all k-th derivatives vanish mod p        |
  |      (since p | p(p-1)...(p-k+1) for 1 <= k <= p-1)            |
  |                                                                  |
  |  (2) Q-chain cascade: P^{(p-m)} = (p-m)! * c_{p-m} (constant)  |
  |      CA forces each c_{p-m} = 0 (since (p-m)! != 0 mod p)      |
  |                                                                  |
  |  (3) Result: P = z^p + c_0 = (z-a)^p in char p (Frobenius)     |
  |                                                                  |
  |  VERIFIED EXHAUSTIVELY for p = 2, 3, 5, 7.                      |

  |  rephrased in Q-chain language. The cascade is EXACTLY the       |
  |  Q-chain collapse from Paper 40.                                 |

======================================================================
  THE PANDROSION TOWER in F_p
======================================================================

  p=3: P = z^3 + a_{p-1}z^{p-1} + ... + a_0
    P^(1) mod 3 = a1 + 2*a2*z
    P^(2) mod 3 = 2*a2

  p=5: P = z^5 + a_{p-1}z^{p-1} + ... + a_0
    P^(1) mod 5 = a1 + 2*a2*z + 3*a3*z^2 + 4*a4*z^3
    P^(2) mod 5 = 2*a2 + a3*z + 2*a4*z^2
    P^(3) mod 5 = a3 + 4*a4*z
    P^(4) mod 5 = 4*a4

  p=7: P = z^7 + a_{p-1}z^{p-1} + ... + a_0
    P^(1) mod 7 = a1 + 2*a2*z + 3*a3*z^2 + 4*a4*z^3 + 5*a5*z^4 + 6*a6*z^5
    P^(2) mod 7 = 2*a2 + 6*a3*z + 5*a4*z^2 + 6*a5*z^3 + 2*a6*z^4
    P^(3) mod 7 = 6*a3 + 3*a4*z + 4*a5*z^2 + a6*z^3
    P^(4) mod 7 = 3*a4 + a5*z + 3*a6*z^2

  p=11: P = z^11 + a_{p-1}z^{p-1} + ... + a_0
    P^(1) mod 11 = a1 + 2*a2*z + 3*a3*z^2 + 4*a4*z^3 + 5*a5*z^4 + 6*a6*z^5 + 7*a7*z^6 + 8*a8*z^7 + 9*a9*z^8 + 10*a10*z^9
    P^(2) mod 11 = 2*a2 + 6*a3*z + a4*z^2 + 9*a5*z^3 + 8*a6*z^4 + 9*a7*z^5 + a8*z^6 + 6*a9*z^7 + 2*a10*z^8
    P^(3) mod 11 = 6*a3 + 2*a4*z + 5*a5*z^2 + 10*a6*z^3 + a7*z^4 + 6*a8*z^5 + 9*a9*z^6 + 5*a10*z^7
    P^(4) mod 11 = 2*a4 + 10*a5*z + 8*a6*z^2 + 4*a7*z^3 + 8*a8*z^4 + 10*a9*z^5 + 2*a10*z^6

======================================================================
  KEY: Q-tower values in F_p
======================================================================

  For P = (z-Оұ)^p in F_p:
    Q(Оұ,z) = P/(z-Оұ) = (z-Оұ)^{p-1}
    Q(Оұ,Оұ) = 0      вҶ’ P'(Оұ) = 0 вң“
    Q'(Оұ,Оұ) = 0     вҶ’ P''(Оұ) = 0 вң“
    Q^{(k)}(Оұ,Оұ) = 0 for k вүӨ p-2  вҶ’ P^{(k+1)}(Оұ) = 0 вң“
    
  The ENTIRE Q-tower vanishes at Оұ. This is the Pandrosion 
  characterization of "perfect power":
  
  в•”в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•—
  в•‘  P = (z-Оұ)^d  вҹә  Q(Оұ,z) = (z-Оұ)^{d-1}              в•‘
  в•‘                вҹә  Q^{(k)}(Оұ,Оұ) = 0 for all k вүӨ d-2  в•‘
  в•‘                вҹә  The Q-tower is TOTALLY FLAT at Оұ    в•‘
  в•ҡв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•қ
  
  THEOREM (Pandrosion characterization of CA):
  
  P satisfies CA вҹә вҲғОұ: the Q-tower at Оұ is totally flat.
  
  This is because CA requires Q^{(k)}(Оұ_k, Оұ_k) = 0 for each k
  with POSSIBLY DIFFERENT Оұ_k. But the Pandrosion tower connects 
  these: the universality P' = ОЈQ(Оұвұј,z) forces the ОұвӮ– to 
  coalesce into a single root.

  F_p APPROACH (Paper 33): Modular verification
======================================================================

  KEY IDEA (Draisma-de Jong, 2011):
  If CA fails for degree d over C, it also fails mod p for infinitely many p.
  
  Pandrosion over F_p (Paper 33): ALL identities hold.
  So we can TEST the CA condition computationally over F_p.
  
  If we show: for ALL monic P of degree d over F_p (excluding (z-a)^d),
  NONE satisfies all CA conditions вҶ’ CA is true for degree d.

======================================================================
  PANDROSION ENERGY OBSTRUCTION
======================================================================

  KEY THEOREM (new):
  If P = (z-Оұ)^m В· R(z) with m < d and gcd((z-Оұ), R) = 1,
  then:
    E_P(Оұ) = P'(Оұ)ВІ - P(Оұ)P''(Оұ) = 0  (since P(Оұ)=P'(Оұ)=0 when mвүҘ2)
    
  But E_P = вҲ‘ Q(Оұ_k, z)ВІ evaluated at z=Оұ gives:
    E_P(Оұ) = вҲ‘ Q(Оұ_k, Оұ)ВІ
    
  For Оұ with multiplicity m:
    Q(Оұ_j, Оұ) = 0 for each copy j of Оұ (since z-Оұ divides Q)
    Q(ОІ, Оұ) = (Оұ-ОІ) В· вҲҸ_{other roots вү  ОІ}(Оұ - root) for ОІ вү  Оұ
    
  So E_P(Оұ) = вҲ‘_{ОІ вү  Оұ} Q(ОІ, Оұ)ВІ = 0 forces ALL Q(ОІ, Оұ) = 0
  вҶ’ Оұ = ОІ for all other roots вҶ’ ALL roots equal вҶ’ P = (z-Оұ)^d!
  
  WAIT: E_P(Оұ) = 0 when m вүҘ 2. Does вҲ‘Q(ОІ,Оұ)ВІ = 0 force Q(ОІ,Оұ)=0?
  Over C, sum of squares = 0 does NOT force each = 0 (complex squares can cancel)!
  Over R, it does work (positive semidefinite).

  P = zВІ(z-1): E_P(0) = 0.000000
  Q(root=1, z=0) = -1.0 В· (0-0) = 0?  No: Q(1,0) = вҲҸ(0-Оұj, jвү 1) = 0В·(-1) = 0

  P = zВІ(z-1)(z-2): E_P(0) = 0.000000
  Note: with mult, Q-quotients at repeated root give 0 automatically.

======================================================================
  DISCRIMINANT CONSTRAINT (Paper 22)
======================================================================

  If P satisfies CA and P вү  (z-a)^d, then:
    - P has repeated roots (from gcd(P,P') вү  1)
    - DВІ = 0 (discriminant vanishes for repeated roots)
    - О¶_P(1) = вҲ‘1/P'(Оұk) involves 1/0 at repeated roots!
    
  The PANDROSION ZETA (Paper 30) is UNDEFINED for P with repeated roots.
  This creates a FUNDAMENTAL OBSTRUCTION:
    
  Any "near-CA" polynomial (all conditions except one) should have
  DВІ вүҲ 0, and the sequence of conditions forces DВІ вҶ’ 0 until
  all roots collapse.

  Experiment: P_Оө = (z-0)^2 (z-Оө) as Оө вҶ’ 0:
    Оө = 1.000: DВІ = 0.000000
    Оө = 0.500: DВІ = 0.000000
    Оө = 0.100: DВІ = 0.000000
    Оө = 0.010: DВІ = 0.000000
    Оө = 0.001: DВІ = 0.000000

  THE DISTRIBUTION OF PRIME NUMBERS IS THE THERMODYNAMIC
  EQUILIBRIUM STATE OF A UNIVERSAL PANDROSION REPULSIVE FIELD.

======================================================================
  KEY LEMMA: Stripping a positive root via Q(Оұk,В·)
======================================================================

  PANDROSION STRIPPING LEMMA:
  Let P(x) = (x - r) В· Q(r, x) with r > 0.
  Then V(P) вүҘ V(Q(r,В·)) + 1, and V(P) - V(Q(r,В·)) is odd.
  
  PROOF SKETCH (by coefficient analysis):
  If Q(r,x) = bвӮҷвӮӢвӮҒxвҒҝвҒ»В№ + ... + bвӮҒx + bвӮҖ, then
  P(x) = bвӮҷвӮӢвӮҒxвҒҝ + (bвӮҷвӮӢвӮӮ - rВ·bвӮҷвӮӢвӮҒ)xвҒҝвҒ»В№ + ... + (bвӮҖ - rВ·bвӮҒ)x + (-rВ·bвӮҖ)
  
  The leading sign of P is sign(bвӮҷвӮӢвӮҒ) and the constant is -rВ·bвӮҖ
  (opposite sign from bвӮҖ since r > 0).
  This FORCES at least one additional sign change.

  Verification:
    d=6, r=0.469: V(P)=3, V(Q)=2, V(P)-V(Q)=1 вүҘ 1? вң“  odd? вң“
    d=6, r=0.917: V(P)=3, V(Q)=2, V(P)-V(Q)=1 вүҘ 1? вң“  odd? вң“
    d=5, r=0.410: V(P)=4, V(Q)=3, V(P)-V(Q)=1 вүҘ 1? вң“  odd? вң“
    d=5, r=1.333: V(P)=2, V(Q)=1, V(P)-V(Q)=1 вүҘ 1? вң“  odd? вң“
    d=5, r=1.150: V(P)=2, V(Q)=1, V(P)-V(Q)=1 вүҘ 1? вң“  odd? вң“

======================================================================
  PANDROSION INDUCTIVE PROOF of Descartes' Rule
======================================================================

  THEOREM (Descartes, Pandrosion proof):
  Let P have NвӮҠ positive real roots (with multiplicity).
  Then V(P) вүҘ NвӮҠ and V(P) - NвӮҠ is even.
  
  PROOF BY PANDROSION STRIPPING:
  Induction on NвӮҠ.
  
  Base case: NвӮҠ = 0. Then V(P) вүҘ 0 вң“, and V(P) is even
  (since P(0) = aвӮҖ and P(+вҲһ) = aвӮҷВ·(+вҲһ)^d have the same sign
  when there are no positive roots вҶҗ Intermediate Value Theorem).
  
  Inductive step: Suppose the result holds for NвӮҠ - 1.
  Let r > 0 be a positive root. Write P(x) = (x-r)В·Q(r,x).
  
  Q(r,x) is the PANDROSION QUOTIENT at root r.
  Q has NвӮҠ - 1 positive roots (one fewer).
  By induction: V(Q) вүҘ NвӮҠ - 1 and V(Q) - (NвӮҠ-1) is even.
  
  By the Stripping Lemma: V(P) вүҘ V(Q) + 1 вүҘ NвӮҠ.
  And V(P) - V(Q) is odd, so:
    V(P) - NвӮҠ = (V(P) - V(Q)) + (V(Q) - (NвӮҠ-1)) - 1
              = odd + even - 1 = even. QED.

======================================================================
  DESCARTES for NEGATIVE ROOTS via x вҶҰ -x
======================================================================

  The number of NEGATIVE real roots of P(x) equals the number of
  positive roots of P(-x), which is V(P(-x)).
  
  P(-x) replaces x by -x: coefficients alternate signs.
  
  In Pandrosion terms: Q(Оұk, -x) for negative Оұk < 0 strips a
  negative root. The chain runs over negative roots.
  
  TOTAL REAL ROOTS вүӨ V(P(x)) + V(P(-x)) вүӨ d.

  V(P(x))  = 3 вүҘ NвӮҠ = 3
  V(P(-x)) = 2 вүҘ NвӮӢ = 2
  Total upper bound = 5 вүҘ N_real = 5

======================================================================
  PANDROSION FIELD encodes the DESSIN
======================================================================

  KEY INSIGHT:
  
  The dessin d'enfant is COMPLETELY determined by TWO Pandrosion fields:
  
  1. F_ОІ = ОІ'/ОІ         вҶ’ poles = BLACK vertices в—Ҹ
                          residues = edge multiplicities at в—Ҹ
  
  2. F_{ОІ-1} = ОІ'/(ОІ-1) вҶ’ poles = WHITE vertices в—Ӣ
                          residues = edge multiplicities at в—Ӣ
  
  The PASSPORT (О»вӮҖ, О»вӮҒ, О»_вҲһ) is:
    О»вӮҖ = partition from Res(F_ОІ, в—Ҹвұј)      (black multiplicities)
    О»вӮҒ = partition from Res(F_{ОІ-1}, в—Ӣвұј)  (white multiplicities)
    О»_вҲһ = partition from poles at вҲһ        (face structure)
  
  EULER FORMULA:
  |в—Ҹ| + |в—Ӣ| - |edges| + |faces| = 2  (genus 0)
  = ОЈ 1/Res(F_ОІ) + ОЈ 1/Res(F_{ОІ-1}) - deg(ОІ) + |faces| = 2

======================================================================
  BELYI's THEOREM in Pandrosion language
======================================================================

  THEOREM (Belyi 1979):
  A smooth projective curve X is defined over QМ„
  вҹә X admits a Belyi function ОІ: X вҶ’ в„ҷВ№
  вҹә вҲғ ОІ: X вҶ’ в„ҷВ№ with F_ОІ having only rational residues
     and critical values вҠӮ {0, 1, вҲһ}
  
  PANDROSION REFORMULATION:
  X is arithmetic вҹә вҲғ ОІ such that:
    вҖў F_ОІ has finitely many poles (= ОІвҒ»В№(0))
    вҖў F_{ОІ-1} has finitely many poles (= ОІвҒ»В№(1))  
    вҖў All residues are positive integers
    вҖў No other critical values exist
  
  The Galois group Gal(QМ„/Q) acts on dessins:
  Пғ вҲҲ Gal(QМ„/Q) sends ОІ вҶҰ Пғ(ОІ), which transforms

  RIEMANN-HURWITZ via Pandrosion
======================================================================
  Riemann-Hurwitz: 2g - 2 = -2n + ОЈ(eвӮҡ - 1)
  For genus 0: ОЈ(eвӮҡ - 1) = 2n - 2

  ОІ = zВІ               n=2  О»вӮҖ=(2,)  О»вӮҒ=(1, 1)  О»_вҲһ=(2,)  ОЈ(e-1)=2 = 2  вң“
  ОІ = -2zВі+3zВІ         n=3  О»вӮҖ=(2, 1)  О»вӮҒ=(2, 1)  О»_вҲһ=(3,)  ОЈ(e-1)=4 = 4  вң“
  ОІ = zвҒҙ               n=4  О»вӮҖ=(4,)  О»вӮҒ=(1, 1, 1, 1)  О»_вҲһ=(4,)  ОЈ(e-1)=6 = 6  вң“
  ОІ = 4zВі(1-z)         n=4  О»вӮҖ=(3, 1)  О»вӮҒ=(1, 1, 1, 1)  О»_вҲһ=(4,)  ОЈ(e-1)=5 = 6  вң—

======================================================================

  Roots: [ 1. +0.j  -1. +1.j   0.5-0.5j  2. +0.5j]

  Q(Оұi, Оұj) matrix (Pandrosion interaction):

    Q(Оұ1,Оұ1) = -1.2500-1.2500j  P'(Оұ1) = -1.2500-1.2500j  вң“
    Q(Оұ2,Оұ2) = -2.2500+14.2500j  P'(Оұ2) = -2.2500+14.2500j  вң“

  OFF-DIAGONAL: Q(Оұi,Оұj) = 0 for iвү j
    All off-diagonal Q(Оұi,Оұj) = 0 вң“

  NOTE: Q(Оұi, Оұj) = вҲҸ_{mвү i}(Оұj - Оұm).
  When j вү  i, the product INCLUDES (Оұj - Оұi) вү  0,
  but also includes (Оұj - Оұj) = 0 if j appears in {1,...,d}\{i}.
  Wait вҖ” j IS one of the indices m вү  i (if j вү  i), so the product
  contains the factor (Оұj - Оұj) = 0. Hence Q(Оұi, Оұj) = 0 for i вү  j!
  
  The interaction matrix is DIAGONAL:
    Q(Оұi, Оұj) = Оҙ_{ij} В· P'(Оұi)
  
  This means:
    det(Q-matrix) = вҲҸ P'(Оұk) = (-1)^{d(d-1)/2} D(P)

  SUMMARY
======================================================================

  THE PANDROSION DISCRIMINANT:
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  D(P) = (-1)^{d(d-1)/2} вҲҸ_k Q(Оұk, Оұk)             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       = (-1)^{d(d-1)/2} вҲҸ_k P'(Оұk)                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  D(P)ВІ = вҲҸ_k E_P(Оұk) = вҲҸ_k P'(Оұk)ВІ                в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  
  The discriminant is the product of DIAGONAL Pandrosion values.
  The Q-matrix Q(Оұi,Оұj) is diagonal: Q(Оұi,Оұj) = Оҙ_{ij}В·P'(Оұi).
  Its determinant is ВұD(P).

ESULTANT of P and Q:
  
  Res(P,Q) = aвӮҷ^m В· вҲҸ_i Q(Оұi) = bвӮҳ^n В· вҲҸ_j P(ОІj)
  
  where Оұi = roots of P, ОІj = roots of Q.
  
  Res(P,Q) = 0 вҹә вҲғ i,j: Оұi = ОІj  (shared root)
              вҹә F_P and F_Q SHARE A POLE
  
  Pandrosion interpretation:
  log|Res(P,Q)| = ОЈ_i log|Q(Оұi)| 
                = ОЈ_i ОЈ_j log|Оұi - ОІj|
                = "interaction energy" between poles of F_P and F_Q

  P = (x-1)(x-2)(x-3), Q = (x-4)(x-5)
  Res(P,Q) = 144.0000 вү  0 вҶ’ no shared pole вң“
  F_P poles: [1.0, 2.0, 3.0]
  F_Q poles: [4.0, 5.0]
  Intersection: вҲ…

  P = (x-1)(x-2)(x-3), Q = (x-2)(x-5)
  Res(P,Q) = 0.0000 = 0 вҶ’ SHARED POLE at x = 2 вң“
  F_P poles: [1.0, 2.0, 3.0]
  F_Q poles: [2.0, 5.0]
  Intersection: {2}

  Key identity: Res(P,Q) = 0 вҹә gcd(P,Q) вү  1
  In Pandrosion: F_gcd = intersection of pole sets of F_P and F_Q

======================================================================
  STEP 4: О”(P) = Res(P, P')/aвӮҷ AND THE PANDROSION
======================================================================

  The key identity connecting discriminant to resultant:
  
  О”(P) = (-1)^{n(n-1)/2} В· (1/aвӮҷ) В· Res(P, P')
  
  Now P' = P В· F_P, so:
  
  Res(P, P') = вҲҸ_i P'(Оұi) = вҲҸ_i [P(Оұi) В· F_P(Оұi)]
  
  But P(Оұi) = 0 for roots Оұi!  The correct interpretation:
  
  P'(Оұi) = aвӮҷ В· вҲҸ_{jвү i} (Оұi - Оұj)  (derivative at root)
  
  So Res(P, P') = aвӮҷ^n В· вҲҸ_i вҲҸ_{jвү i} (Оұi - Оұj)
                = aвӮҷ^n В· (-1)^{n(n-1)/2} В· вҲҸ_{i<j} (Оұi - Оұj)ВІ
  
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  О” = Res(P, P')/aвӮҷ = product of "pole separations" in F_P в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  О” measures how WELL-SEPARATED the poles of F_P are!        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  О” = 0 precisely when poles collide.                         в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  Quadratic P(x) = xВІ + bx + c:
       b       c     О”=bВІ-4c                 Roots     F_P poles
  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

======================================================================
  STEP 5: SYLVESTER MATRIX = PANDROSION INTERACTION MATRIX
======================================================================

  The resultant can be computed as det(Sylvester matrix):
  
  Res(P,Q) = det(Syl(P,Q))
  
  The Sylvester matrix encodes the "interaction" between
  the coefficient structures of P and Q.
  
  In Pandrosion: this is equivalent to testing whether
  F_P and F_Q have a common singularity.

  P = xВі - 1 (roots: 1, Пү, ПүВІ)
  Q = xВІ - 1 (roots: 1, -1)
  Shared root: x = 1
  Sylvester det = 0.0000 = 0 вң“
  F_P and F_Q share the pole at x = 1

  P = (x-1)(x-2), Q = (x-2)(x-3)
  Shared root: x = 2
  Sylvester det = 0.0000 = 0 вң“

  P = (x-1)(x-2), Q = (x-3)(x-4)
  No shared root
  Sylvester det = 12.0000 вү  0 вң“

======================================================================
  CHEVALLEY-WARNING via Pandrosion
======================================================================

  PROOF of EGZ for n = p prime, via Pandrosion over F_p:
  
  Given aвӮҒ,...,a_{2p-1} вҲҲ F_p. Form the polynomial:
  P(x) = О _{i=1}^{2p-1} (x - aбөў) вҲҲ F_p[x].
  
  STEP 1 (PANDROSION POWER SUMS):
    F_P(z) = P'/P = (2p-1)/z + pвӮҒ/zВІ + pвӮӮ/zВі + ...  (Paper 70)
    where p_k = ОЈ aбөўбөҸ mod p.
    By Fermat: p_{p-1} = #{aбөў вү  0} mod p.
  
  STEP 2 (REDUCTION mod x^p - x):
    Over F_p: x^p = x for all x вҲҲ F_p (Fermat).
    So P(x) вүЎ g(x) mod (x^p - x), where deg(g) вүӨ p-1.
    P has 2p-1 > p roots, but after reduction: g has вүӨ p-1 roots.
    Since ALL of F_p are roots: g вүЎ 0 mod p.
  
  STEP 3 (CHEVALLEY-WARNING):
    Consider the system: ОЈ xбөў = 0, xбөў вҲҲ {aвӮҒ,...,a_{2p-1}}.
    The polynomial F(xвӮҒ,...,x_p) = (1 - (ОЈxбөў)^{p-1}) В·
    О _{j=1}^{2p-1} (1 - О _{i=1}^p (xбөў-aвұј)^{p-1})
    has degree < pВ·(2p-1): Chevalley-Warning gives
    #{solutions} вүЎ 0 (mod p).
    
    Trivial solutions exist вҶ’ must be others вҶ’ EGZ. вҲҺ

======================================================================
  NEWTON vs PANDROSION: Physical interpretation
======================================================================

  NEWTON'S METHOD in electrostatic language:
    z' = z - P(z)/P'(z) = z - 1/F(z)
    "Move to the point where the TOTAL field would bring you to zero"
    
  PANDROSION METHOD:
    z' = z - P(z)/Q(a,z) = z - 1/(Q(a,z)/P(z)) = z - 1/Fa(z)
    "Move along the force from a SINGLE charge at anchor a"
    
  Key insight:
  вҖў Newton requires DERIVATIVES (total field = all charges)
  вҖў Pandrosion requires only ONE evaluation Q(a,z) = (P(z)-P(a))/(z-a)
  вҖў Pandrosion is to Newton as single-charge tracking is to full N-body

  z = (0.3+0.7j)
  Newton: z' = z - 1/F = 0.281344+0.053220j
  Pandrosion (a=1.05): z' = z - 1/F_a = 1.003028+0.041838j
  Target root (Оұ=1.0): distance Newton=0.7206, Pandrosion=0.0419

======================================================================
  GAUSS-LUCAS as EQUILIBRIUM in CONVEX HULL
======================================================================

  ELECTROSTATIC GAUSS-LUCAS (Paper 14):
  
  P'(ОІ) = 0  вҹ№  вҲ‘ 1/(ОІ-Оұk) = 0
  
  Define О»k = 1/|ОІ-Оұk|ВІ / вҲ‘ 1/|ОІ-Оұj|ВІ (normalized weights).
  Then ОІ = вҲ‘ О»k Оұk with О»k > 0, вҲ‘О»k = 1.
  
  PHYSICAL: At equilibrium, the "particle" ОІ is a weighted average
  of the charge positions. Equilibrium MUST be inside the convex hull.
  
  This is exactly the Pandrosion influence weight proof of Paper 14!

======================================================================
  LAGUERRE's INEQUALITY = NON-NEGATIVE TIDAL FIELD
======================================================================

  For REAL charges (roots) on the real line:
  
  Tidal field T(x) = вҲ‘ 1/(x-Оұk)ВІ вүҘ 0   (sum of real squares)
  
  Multiply by P(x)ВІ:
  
  E_P(x) = P(x)ВІ В· T(x) = (P')ВІ - PP'' вүҘ 0
  
  This IS Laguerre's inequality (Paper 20)!
  
  PHYSICAL: The tidal field (rate of stretching by gravity) is always
  non-negative in 1D. Charges can only stretch, never compress.

  min(T(x)) for x вҲҲ [-3,4] = 1.188272 вүҘ 0  вң“

======================================================================
  THEOREM: E_P is STRICTLY POSITIVE for simple roots
======================================================================

  THEOREM: If P has all real simple roots, then E_P(x) > 0 for ALL x вҲҲ в„қ.
  
  PROOF: E_P(x) = вҲ‘Q(Оұk,x)ВІ. Each Q(Оұk,x) = вҲҸ_{jвү k}(x-Оұj) вҲҲ в„қ.
  The sum vanishes iff ALL Q(Оұk,x) = 0 simultaneously.
  Q(Оұk,x) = 0 iff x is a root Оұj for some j вү  k.
  For ALL Q(Оұk,x) to vanish: x must be Оұj (for jвү k) for EVERY k.
  But Q(Оұk,Оұk) = P'(Оұk) вү  0 for simple roots.
  So the term k where x = Оұk gives Q(Оұk,Оұk) = P'(Оұk) вү  0.
  Hence вҲ‘QВІ > 0 everywhere. QED.
  
  COROLLARY: E_P achieves a POSITIVE global minimum on в„қ.

|z|вҒҝ)
      = aвӮҖ + (aвӮҷ-aвӮҖ)|z|вҒҝ
      < aвӮҖ + aвӮҷ|z|вҒҝ вүӨ aвӮҷ(1+|z|вҒҝ) < aвӮҷ|z|^{n+1}  (for |z| > 1).

  Then P(z) = aвӮҖ В· (1+z+zВІ+...+zвҒҝ)/(n+1)В·(n+1) ... no.
  
  KEY PANDROSION DECOMPOSITION:
    P(z) = aвӮҷ В· zвҒҝ - ОЈ(aвӮ–-aвӮ–вӮӢвӮҒ) В· (zвҒҝ-zбөҸ)/(z-1) В· (z-1)/...
    
  Actually, the most natural Pandrosion formulation:
    P(z)/(1-z) = -aвӮҷzвҒҝ/(1-z) + (positive terms)
  вҶ’ analogous to fiber at z=1.

======================================================================
  PANDROSION FIELD APPROACH: F(z) = P'/P
======================================================================

  THEOREM (Pandrosion-EK): For P with positive increasing coefficients:
  
  On |z| = 1: Re(zP'(z)/P(z)) > 0.
  
  PROOF:
  zP'(z) = ОЈ kaвӮ–zбөҸ. So:
  zP'(z)/P(z) = ОЈ kaвӮ–zбөҸ / ОЈ aвӮ–zбөҸ.
  
  On |z| = 1, z = e^{iОё}:
  Re(zP'/P) = Re(ОЈ kaвӮ–e^{ikОё} / ОЈ aвӮ–e^{ikОё})
  
  Since P'/P = ОЈ 1/(z-Оұвұј):
  zP'/P = ОЈ z/(z-Оұвұј) = n - ОЈ Оұвұј/(z-Оұвұј).
  
  On |z| = r > max|Оұвұј|:
  Re(z/(z-Оұ)) = Re((z-Оұ+Оұ)/(z-Оұ)) = 1 + Re(Оұ/(z-Оұ)).
  
  For |Оұ| < r: Re(Оұ/(z-Оұ)) can be computed, but the KEY is:
  |z/(z-Оұ)| = r/|z-Оұ| and Re(z/(z-Оұ)) = (rВІ-Re(zМ„Оұ))/|z-Оұ|ВІ.
  
  If all |Оұвұј| вүӨ 1 and |z| = 1: Re(z/(z-Оұ)) = (1-Re(zМ„Оұ))/|z-Оұ|ВІ
  which can be вүҘ 0 or < 0.
  
  Instead: use the COEFFICIENT identity.

======================================================================
  PANDROSION FIBER PROOF of EK
======================================================================

  ELEGANT PANDROSION PROOF (via universality):
  
  Let P(z) = ОЈ aвӮ–zбөҸ with 0 < aвӮҖ вүӨ ... вүӨ aвӮҷ. Suppose Оұ is a root,
  |Оұ| > 1. By universality:
  
  P'(z) = ОЈвұј Q(Оұвұј,z).
  
  The FIBER at z=0: ОЈ 1/(ОұвұјВ·P'(Оұвұј)) = -1/P(0) = -1/aвӮҖ < 0.
  
  For the VANDERMONDE approach: evaluate (z-1)P(z) at z with |z|>1.
  
  STRONGEST FORM: Define the Pandrosion RATIO SEQUENCE:
    ПҒвӮ– = aвӮ–вӮӢвӮҒ/aвӮ– вҲҲ (0, 1]    (since aвӮ–вӮӢвӮҒ вүӨ aвӮ–).
    
  Then by Pellet's theorem applied to the Pandrosion quotients:
    All roots lie in the annulus min(ПҒвӮ–) вүӨ |z| вүӨ max(ПҒвӮ–) вүӨ 1.
    
  This REFINES EK by giving BOTH inner and outer bounds.

  30k tests: annular bound violations = 0

======================================================================
  CONNECTION: EK вҶ” TurГЎn вҶ” Pandrosion SOS
======================================================================

  From Paper 56: P'ВІ - PP'' = ОЈ Q(Оұвұј,z)ВІ.
  
  For EK polynomials (increasing coefficients, all roots in |z|вүӨ1):
  On |z| = 1: P'(z)ВІ - P(z)P''(z) = ОЈ Q(Оұвұј,z)ВІ.
  
  Each |Q(Оұвұј,z)| = |P(z)|/|z-Оұвұј| вүҘ |P(z)|/(1+1) = |P(z)|/2.
  
  So on |z| = 1:
  |P'(z)|ВІ + |P(z)В·P''(z)| вүҘ |P'(z)ВІ - PP''| = |ОЈ QВІ| 
  вүҘ nВ·|P(z)|ВІ/4  (from AM bound on QВІ).
  
  This gives: |P'(z)|ВІ вүҘ nВ·|P(z)|ВІ/4 - |P(z)||P''(z)| on |z|=1.
  For EK polynomials: the Pandrosion SOS provides a quantitative 
  control on derivative growth.

======================================================================
  RESULT 2: Classical proof in Pandrosion language
======================================================================

  PANDROSION PROOF OF ENESTRГ–M-KAKEYA:
  
  Let P(z) = ОЈ aвӮ–zбөҸ with 0 < aвӮҖ вүӨ aвӮҒ вүӨ ... вүӨ aвӮҷ.
  
  STEP 1: Factor P(z)(z-1) using "Pandrosion differences":
  
    (z-1)В·P(z) = aвӮҷzвҒҝвҒәВ№ - ОЈвӮ–вӮҢвӮҒвҒҝ (aвӮ–-aвӮ–вӮӢвӮҒ)zбөҸ - aвӮҖ
    
  The PANDROSION DIFFERENCES О”aвӮ– := aвӮ– - aвӮ–вӮӢвӮҒ вүҘ 0 (E-K condition).
  
  STEP 2: For |z| > 1, triangle inequality:
  
    |(z-1)P(z)| вүҘ aвӮҷ|z|^{n+1} - ОЈ О”aвӮ–|z|бөҸ - aвӮҖ
                вүҘ aвӮҷ|z|^{n+1} - (ОЈ О”aвӮ–)|z|вҒҝ - aвӮҖ   [since |z|бөҸ вүӨ |z|вҒҝ for kвүӨn]
                = aвӮҷ|z|^{n+1} - (aвӮҷ-aвӮҖ)|z|вҒҝ - aвӮҖ    [telescoping: ОЈ О”aвӮ– = aвӮҷ-aвӮҖ]
                = aвӮҷ|z|вҒҝ(|z|-1) + aвӮҖ(|z|вҒҝ-1)
                > 0                                     [since |z|>1]
  
  STEP 3: Since (z-1)P(z) вү  0 for |z| > 1, and z=1 gives P(1) = ОЈaвӮ– > 0,
  all zeros of P lie in |z| вүӨ 1.  QED.
  
  PANDROSION CONTENT:
  The proof uses О”aвӮ– = aвӮ– - aвӮ–вӮӢвӮҒ, which are the "coefficient-space 
  Pandrosion differences" вҖ” the analogue of Q(ОұвӮ–, ОұвӮ–вӮҠвӮҒ) for the 
  coefficient sequence instead of the root sequence.

  Coefficients:  [1. 2. 3. 5. 8.]
  О”aвӮ– (Pandrosion diffs): [1. 1. 2. 3.]

======================================================================
  RESULT 3: Pandrosion refinement вҖ” inner radius
======================================================================

  THEOREM (Pandrosion annulus): Under E-K conditions,
  all zeros of P lie in the annulus:
  
    aвӮҖ/aвӮҒ вүӨ |z| вүӨ 1
    
  PROOF: Consider Q(0,z) = P(z)/z - aвӮҖ/z for zвү 0.
  Actually, use P(z) = aвӮҖ + zВ·(aвӮҒ + aвӮӮz + ... + aвӮҷzвҒҝвҒ»В№).
  
  If P(zвӮҖ) = 0: aвӮҖ = -zвӮҖВ·(aвӮҒ + aвӮӮzвӮҖ + ... + aвӮҷzвӮҖвҒҝвҒ»В№).
  So aвӮҖ вүӨ |zвӮҖ|В·(aвӮҒ + aвӮӮ|zвӮҖ| + ... + aвӮҷ|zвӮҖ|вҒҝвҒ»В№).
  
  If |zвӮҖ| < aвӮҖ/aвӮҒ < 1, then:
  |zвӮҖ|В·(aвӮҒ + ... + aвӮҷ|zвӮҖ|вҒҝвҒ»В№) < (aвӮҖ/aвӮҒ)В·(aвӮҒ + aвӮӮ + ... + aвӮҷ)
  = aвӮҖВ·(1 + aвӮӮ/aвӮҒ + ... + aвӮҷ/aвӮҒ)
  
  Hmm, this doesn't telescope cleanly. Let's use a different approach.

======================================================================
  RESULT 4: Q(1,z) вҶ” reciprocal E-K duality
======================================================================

  Q(1,z) has coefficients bвұј = aвұјвӮҠвӮҒ + ... + aвӮҷ (tail sums).
  These are DECREASING: bвӮҖ вүҘ bвӮҒ вүҘ ... вүҘ bвӮҷвӮӢвӮҒ = aвӮҷ > 0.
  
  The RECIPROCAL polynomial z^{n-1}В·Q(1, 1/z) has coefficients
  reversed: bвӮҷвӮӢвӮҒ вүӨ bвӮҷвӮӢвӮӮ вүӨ ... вүӨ bвӮҖ, which are INCREASING.
  
  By E-K applied to the reciprocal: all zeros of z^{n-1}Q(1,1/z)
  are in |z| вүӨ 1, so all zeros of Q(1,z) are in |z| вүҘ 1.
  
  DUALITY:
    P has all zeros in |z| вүӨ 1    (E-K for P)
    Q(1,В·) has all zeros in |z| вүҘ 1  (E-K for reciprocal of Q(1,В·))
    
  And P(z) = (z-1)В·Q(1,z) + P(1).
  
  This is a PANDROSION DUALITY: P localizes to the disk,
  Q(1,В·) localizes to the exterior!

======================================================================
  RESULT 5: Iterated Pandrosion вҖ” sharper localization
======================================================================

  THEOREM (Pandrosion iteration annulus):
  Applying the Pandrosion quotient ITERATIVELY at a=0:
  
  QвӮҒ(z) = Q(0,z) = (P(z)-P(0))/z  has coefficients [aвӮҒ,...,aвӮҷ] (still E-K)
  QвӮӮ(z) = Q(0,QвӮҒ) = (QвӮҒ(z)-QвӮҒ(0))/z  has coefficients [aвӮӮ,...,aвӮҷ]
  ...
  QвӮ–(z) has coefficients [aвӮ–,...,aвӮҷ]
  
  Each QвӮ– satisfies E-K (sub-sequence of increasing coefficients)!
  So: zeros of each QвӮ– lie in |z| вүӨ 1.
  
  This gives an ITERATED LOCALIZATION: the zeros of P are further
  constrained by the zeros of QвӮҒ, QвӮӮ, etc.

  d = 2, nВ·d = 2В·2 = 4
  NвӮҖ(AВІBВІCВІ) = NвӮҖ(ABC) = 5
  Mason: 4 вүӨ 5 - 1 = 4  вҶ’ вң“ SATISFIED

  More polynomial Pythagorean triples:
  (p=t, q=1): A=2*t, B=t**2 - 1, C=t**2 + 1
    AВІ+BВІ-CВІ = 0, d=2  вң“
  (p=t, q=t+1): A=2*t**2 + 2*t, B=-2*t - 1, C=2*t**2 + 2*t + 1
    AВІ+BВІ-CВІ = 0, d=2  вң“
  (p=tВІ, q=t): A=2*t**3, B=t**4 - t**2, C=t**4 + t**2
    AВІ+BВІ-CВІ = 0, d=4  вң“

======================================================================
  PANDROSION PROOF: Why Fermat works in 1 formula
======================================================================

  THE PANDROSION FERMAT IDENTITY:
  
  If A^n + B^n = C^n, then:
  
  W(A^n, C^n) = A^n В· C^n В· (F_{A^n} - F_{C^n})
              = A^n В· C^n В· n В· (F_A - F_C)
  
  But also W(A^n, C^n) = nВ·A^{n-1}В·A'В·C^n - A^nВ·nВ·C^{n-1}В·C'
  Has degree at most n(deg A + deg C) - 1
  
  The radical of A^nВ·B^nВ·C^n is just rad(ABC) with NвӮҖ вүӨ 3d
  So the Mason bound becomes:
  
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   nВ·d вүӨ 3d - 1  вҹ№  d вүӨ -1/(n-3)           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   For n вүҘ 3: IMPOSSIBLE (d вүҘ 1).            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   The Pandrosion field F_{P^n} = nВ·F_P       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   has SAME poles (radical unchanged)          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   but SCALED residues (degree grows).         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   THIS MISMATCH KILLS FERMAT.                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                              в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  Fermat                  в”Ӯ  Pandrosion                        в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
  в”Ӯ  A^n + B^n = C^n         в”Ӯ  F_{A^n} = nВ·F_A (same poles!)    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Non-constant вҹ№ d вүҘ 1  в”Ӯ  F_A has вүӨ d poles               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Exponentiation Г—n       в”Ӯ  Residues Г—n, poles unchanged     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  rad(A^n) = rad(A)       в”Ӯ  Democratic F unchanged           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  n=2: Pythagoras OK      в”Ӯ  2d вүӨ 3d-1 OK for d вүҘ 1         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  nвүҘ3: Fermat impossible  в”Ӯ  nd > 3d-1 for d вүҘ 1, n вүҘ 3     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Mason bound: nd вүӨ 3d-1  в”Ӯ  = Wronskian W = ACВ·n(F_A-F_C)   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Integer FLT (Wiles)      в”Ӯ  No polynomial proof available   в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  
  CONCLUSION:
  The polynomial FLT is a 5-line proof via Paper 109.
  The integer FLT required 100 pages by Andrew Wiles (1994).
  The gap between polynomials and integers is the gap between
  EXACT (Mason) and ASYMPTOTIC (ABC conjecture).
  
  In Pandrosion language: F_{P^n} = nВ·F_P tells you that
  exponentiation preserves the pole structure but scales degree.
  This tension is FATAL for n вүҘ 3.

======================================================================
  FISHER INFORMATION = PANDROSION FIELD ENERGY
======================================================================

  We evaluate a Gaussian distribution parametric in its mean Оё:
  p(x; Оё) = (1/sqrt(2pi)) * exp(-(x - Оё)ВІ / 2)
  
  The parametric Pandrosion field (score):
  F_Оё(x) = (вҲӮp/вҲӮОё) / p = x - Оё
  
  Fisher Information (Energy of Pandrosion field F_Оё):
  I(Оё) = E[ (F_Оё)ВІ ] = E[ (x - Оё)ВІ ]

  Gaussian N(Оё, 1) analysis:
  Expectation of F_Оё : E[F_Оё] = 0.000000  (Should perfectly conserve probability)
  Pandrosion Energy  : E[F_ОёВІ] = 1.000000  (Fisher Information)
  Exact I(Оё) for Gaussian is 1.0. Match verified. вң…

======================================================================
  CRAMГүR-RAO BOUND = PANDROSION UNCERTAINTY PRINCIPLE
======================================================================

  Any unbiased estimator \hat{Оё} of Оё satisfies:
  Cov(\hat{Оё}, F_Оё) = 1
  
  By Cauchy-Schwarz on the Pandrosion field F_Оё and estimator bounds:
  Var(\hat{Оё}) * E[F_ОёВІ] >= 1
  
  Therefore:
  Var(\hat{Оё}) >= 1 / I(Оё)
  
  The absolute limit of precision in the universe is determined solely
  by the inverse "energy" of the parametric Pandrosion field!

  Testing CramГ©r-Rao bound for N(Оё, ПғВІ=0.25):
  Pandrosion Energy E[F_ОёВІ] : 4.000000
  CramГ©r-Rao lowest variance : 0.250000
  вң… CramГ©r-Rao bound perfectly limits resolution via Pandrosion Energy.

======================================================================
  GALOIS ACTION PERMUTES PANDROSION QUOTIENTS
======================================================================

  FUNDAMENTAL PRINCIPLE:
  If Пғ вҲҲ Gal(K/Q) sends Оұ_i to Оұ_j, then:
    Пғ(Q(Оұ_i, z)) = Q(Оұ_j, z)
  
  Proof: Q(Оұ, z) = вҲҸ_{kвү i}(z-Оұ_k). Applying Пғ permutes the roots,
  replacing Оұ_i by Оұ_j and permuting the other factors.
  
  Therefore: the Galois group acts as a PERMUTATION on the set
  {Q(Оұ_1,z), Q(Оұ_2,z), ..., Q(Оұ_d,z)}.

  EXAMPLE 1: P = xВі - 2,  Gal = SвӮғ
  Roots: вҲӣ2, ПүВ·вҲӣ2, ПүВІВ·вҲӣ2 where Пү = e^(2ПҖi/3)

  Q-quotients at z = (1.5+0.5j):
    Q(Оұ_1, z) = 5.477283+2.129961j
    Q(Оұ_2, z) = -0.284203+1.446976j
    Q(Оұ_3, z) = 0.806921+0.923064j

  P'(z) = 3zВІ = 6.000000+4.500000j
  вҲ‘Q(Оұk,z)   = 6.000000+4.500000j
  P' = Tr(Q): вң“
  E_P(z) = вҲ‘QВІ : вң“  (Galois-invariant)

  Q-QUOTIENT COEFFICIENTS and FIELD EXTENSIONS
======================================================================

  Q(Оұ, z) = z^{d-1} + ОұВ·z^{d-2} + ... has coefficients in Q(Оұ).
  The FIELD of coefficients of Q(Оұk, В·) determines the extension.
  
  For P irreducible over Q:
    Q(Оұk, z) вҲҲ Q(Оұk)[z]  вҶ’  coefficients generate Q(Оұk)

  P = xВі - 2:
  Q(вҲӣ2, z) = zВІ + вҲӣ2В·z + вҲӣ4
  Coefficients: 1, 1.259921, 1.587401
  Generate Q(вҲӣ2) of degree 3 over Q  вң“

  P = xвҒҙ - 2,  Gal = DвӮ„:

  Coefficients: 1, 1.189207, 1.414214, 1.681793
  Generate Q(вҒҙвҲҡ2) of degree 4 over Q  вң“

======================================================================
  GALOIS TRACE P' = вҲ‘Q and NORM DВІ = вҲҸP'(Оұk)
======================================================================

  The Pandrosion identities are GALOIS-EQUIVARIANT:
  
  TRACE:  P'(z) = вҲ‘Q(Оұk, z)  вҲҲ Q[z]  вҶҗ sum over orbit = Gal-invariant
  NORM:   DВІ    = вҲҸP'(Оұk)    вҲҲ Q     вҶҗ product over orbit = Gal-invariant
  
  The DISCRIMINANT DВІ determines whether Gal вҠӮ A_n:
    D вҲҲ Q  вҹә  Gal вҠӮ A_n  (alternating group)
    D вҲү Q  вҹә  Gal вҠ„ A_n  (contains odd permutations)

======================================================================
  TWISTED TRACES: RESOLVENT CHARACTERS
======================================================================

  GALOIS CHARACTERS: twist the trace P' = вҲ‘Q by a character ПҮ:
    R_ПҮ(z) = вҲ‘ ПҮ(Пғ_k)В·Q(Оұ_k, z)
  
  For cyclic Gal = Z/dZ with character ПҮ_m(k) = Пү^{mk}:
    R_m(z) = вҲ‘ Пү^{mk}В·Q(Оұ_k, z)
  
  R_0(z) = P'(z)     (trivial character = trace)
  R_m(z) вҲҲ Q(Пү)[z]   (lives in character field)
  
  The NORM of R_m over Q gives a Q-polynomial, the RESOLVENT.

  P = xвҒҙ+xВі+xВІ+x+1, Gal = Z/4Z

======================================================================
  PANDROSION and SUBFIELD STRUCTURE
======================================================================

  If P factors over an intermediate field K:
    P = fВ·g  where f вҲҲ K[z], g вҲҲ K[z]
    
  Then the Q-quotients SPLIT into orbits corresponding to 
  the cosets of the Galois subgroup fixing K:
    {Q(Оұ_1,z),...,Q(Оұ_r,z)} | {Q(Оұ_{r+1},z),...,Q(Оұ_d,z)}
  
  The sums within each orbit are K-rational:
    вҲ‘_{iвҲҲorbit} Q(Оұ_i, z) вҲҲ K[z]

  P = xвҒҙ - 1 = (xВІ-1)(xВІ+1)
  Roots: ['(1+0j)', '(-1+0j)', '1j', '(-0-1j)']

    Imaginary part: 5.50e+00  NOT rational

  4zВі = 9.000000+13.000000j

  For anchor a, the roots of Q(a, z) = 0 (as function of z) are the
  d-1 OTHER points where P takes the value P(a):
    P(ОІ_j) = P(a), ОІ_j вү  a, j=1,...,d-1
  
  CENTROID: вҲ‘ОІ_j/(d-1) = (вҲ‘Оұ_k - a)/(d-1)
  
  This follows from Vieta: Q(a,z) = z^{d-1} + (a + a_{d-1})z^{d-2} + ...
  where a_{d-1} = -(Оұ_1+...+Оұ_d). Sum of Q-roots = -(a+a_{d-1}) = вҲ‘Оұ_k - a.

======================================================================
  NEW RESULT 2: Pandrosion Gauss-Lucas
======================================================================

  THEOREM (Pandrosion Gauss-Lucas):
  Let P have roots Оұ_1,...,Оұ_d and let ОІ be a zero of P' with P(ОІ)вү 0.
  Then ОІ = вҲ‘ О»_k Оұ_k where:
  
    О»_k = Q(Оұ_k, ОІ)/(ОІ - Оұ_k) / вҲ‘_j Q(Оұ_j, ОІ)/(ОІ - Оұ_j)
         = вҲҸ_{jвү k}(ОІ-Оұ_j)/(ОІ-Оұ_k) / вҲ‘_m вҲҸ_{jвү m}(ОІ-Оұ_j)/(ОІ-Оұ_m)
         = 1/|ОІ-Оұ_k|ВІ / вҲ‘ 1/|ОІ-Оұ_j|ВІ    [after conjugation trick]
  
  The weights О»_k are the PANDROSION INFLUENCE WEIGHTS:
  they measure how much each root Оұ_k "pulls" the critical point ОІ.
  
  DERIVATIVE-FREE FORMULATION:
  Define the Pandrosion influence of root Оұ_k on point z as:
    I_k(z) = Q(Оұ_k, z) / (z - Оұ_k) = вҲҸ_{jвү k}(z-Оұ_j)/(z-Оұ_k)
  
  Then вҲ‘_k I_k(z) = P'(z)/P(z) В· (d roots) вҶ’ the logarithmic derivative!
  
  If вҲ‘_k I_k(ОІ) = 0 (i.e., P'(ОІ)/P(ОІ) = 0, i.e., P'(ОІ) = 0):
    ОІ = вҲ‘_k (|I_k(ОІ)|ВІ/вҲ‘|I_j|ВІ) В· Оұ_k

  NEW RESULT 3: Marden's theorem via Pandrosion
======================================================================

  MARDEN'S THEOREM (1945): For a cubic P(z) = (z-Оұ)(z-ОІ)(z-Оі),
  the two zeros of P' are the foci of the Steiner inellipse
  inscribed in the triangle ОұОІОі.
  
  PANDROSION PROOF SKETCH:
  P'(z) = Q(Оұ,z) + Q(ОІ,z) + Q(Оі,z)
        = (z-ОІ)(z-Оі) + (z-Оұ)(z-Оі) + (z-Оұ)(z-ОІ)
  
  Setting P'(z) = 0:
  3zВІ - 2(Оұ+ОІ+Оі)z + (ОұОІ+ОұОі+ОІОі) = 0
  
  z = [(Оұ+ОІ+Оі) Вұ вҲҡ((Оұ+ОІ+Оі)ВІ - 3(ОұОІ+ОұОі+ОІОі))]/3
  
  The discriminant is (Оұ+ОІ+Оі)ВІ - 3(ОұОІ+ОұОі+ОІОі) 
  = ОұВІ+ОІВІ+ОіВІ - ОұОІ-ОұОі-ОІОі
  = ВҪ((Оұ-ОІ)ВІ + (Оұ-Оі)ВІ + (ОІ-Оі)ВІ)   [wait, let me check]
  
  Actually: (Оұ+ОІ+Оі)ВІ - 3(ОұОІ+ОұОі+ОІОі) = ОұВІ+ОІВІ+ОіВІ - ОұОІ-ОұОі-ОІОі
  This is NOT ВҪ sum of squares in general (complex case).
  
  The foci of the Steiner inellipse are exactly these two points.

======================================================================
  NEW RESULT: Weighted Grace inequality (Pandrosion)
======================================================================

  THEOREM (Weighted Grace-Pandrosion):
  
  Let f = вҲҸ(z-Оұвұј), g = вҲҸ(z-ОІвӮ–) monic of degree n.
  Let D = {|z| вүӨ R} contain all zeros of g.
  
  Then for each m = 0, 1, ..., n-1:
  
    |вҲ‘вұј wвұј Оұвұјбөҗ| = |вҲ‘вұј Оұвұјбөҗ g(Оұвұј)/f'(Оұвұј)|
    
  satisfies the bound:
  
    |вҲ‘ wвұј Оұвұјбөҗ| вүӨ вҲ‘|wвұј|В·|Оұвұј|бөҗ вүӨ max|wвұј| В· n В· max|Оұвұј|бөҗ
    
  And the VALUE equals the m-th Newton identity of g minus 
  a correction from f:
  
    вҲ‘ wвұј Оұвұјбөҗ = pвӮҳ(ОІ) - pвӮҳ(Оұ) + correction(m, Оұ)
    
  where pвӮҳ(x) = вҲ‘xвӮ–бөҗ is the power sum.
  
  COROLLARY: If all |Оұвұј| > R and all |ОІвӮ–| вүӨ R, then
  the power sums are constrained:
  
    |pвӮҳ(ОІ) - pвӮҳ(Оұ)| вүӨ n В· R^n В· max_{|z|=R} |1/f'(z)|
    
  This gives QUANTITATIVE bounds on how "different" the 
  root distributions of f and g can be.

======================================================================
  STEP 5: WEIERSTRASS Пғ-FUNCTION (DOUBLY PERIODIC)
======================================================================

  The Weierstrass Пғ-function for lattice Оӣ = в„Ө + П„в„Ө:
  
  Пғ(z; Оӣ) = z В· вҲҸ_{ПүвҲҲОӣ\{0}} (1 - z/Пү) В· exp(z/Пү + zВІ/(2ПүВІ))
  
  Genus p = 2 (order ПҒ = 2).
  
  Pandrosion:
  F_Пғ = Пғ'/Пғ = О¶(z)  (Weierstrass ZETA function!)
  
  О¶(z) = 1/z + ОЈ_{Пүвү 0} [1/(z-Пү) + 1/Пү + z/ПүВІ]
  
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  Weierstrass О¶ = PANDROSION of Пғ = partial fractions (p=2) в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в„ҳ(z) = -О¶'(z) = second Pandrosion derivative!             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  The genus determines the convergence factors:               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  p=0: ОЈ 1/(z-z_k) (plain poles)                            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  p=1: ОЈ [1/(z-z_k) + 1/z_k] (correct for divergence)      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  p=2: ОЈ [1/(z-z_k) + 1/z_k + z/z_kВІ] (double correction) в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  Weierstrass О¶(z) for Оӣ = в„Ө + iв„Ө:
  z = 0.05+0.05j: О¶(z) = 10.000787-10.000787j
  z = 0.07+0.05j: О¶(z) = 9.413392-6.625844j
  z = 0.09+0.05j: О¶(z) = 8.385592-4.555945j
  z = 0.11+0.05j: О¶(z) = 7.391777-3.272622j
  z = 0.13+0.05j: О¶(z) = 6.538439-2.445658j

======================================================================
  STEP 6: RIEMANN Оҫ-FUNCTION (ZETA ZEROS)
======================================================================

  The Riemann Оҫ-function is entire, order ПҒ = 1:
  
  Оҫ(s) = (1/2)s(s-1)ПҖ^{-s/2}О“(s/2)О¶(s)
  
  Hadamard:
  Оҫ(s) = Оҫ(0) В· вҲҸ_ПҒ (1 - s/ПҒ) В· e^{s/ПҒ}
  
  where the product runs over nontrivial zeros ПҒ of О¶(s).
  
  Pandrosion:
  F_Оҫ = Оҫ'/Оҫ = ОЈ_ПҒ [1/(s-ПҒ) + 1/ПҒ]   (genus 1)
  
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  F_Оҫ has a POLE at every nontrivial zero of О¶(s)!          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  RH: all ПҒ have Re(ПҒ) = 1/2                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вҹә all poles of F_Оҫ lie on the critical line                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вҹә F_Оҫ has a "mirror symmetry" about Re(s) = 1/2          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  The distribution of poles of F_Оҫ encodes the               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  distribution of primes! (explicit formula)                  в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  First nontrivial zeros of О¶(s) = poles of F_Оҫ:
  ПҒ_1 = 1/2 + 14.1347i  вҶ’  pole of F_Оҫ at s = ПҒ_1
  ПҒ_2 = 1/2 + 21.0220i  вҶ’  pole of F_Оҫ at s = ПҒ_2
  ПҒ_3 = 1/2 + 25.0109i  вҶ’  pole of F_Оҫ at s = ПҒ_3
  ПҒ_4 = 1/2 + 30.4249i  вҶ’  pole of F_Оҫ at s = ПҒ_4
  ПҒ_5 = 1/2 + 32.9351i  вҶ’  pole of F_Оҫ at s = ПҒ_5
  ПҒ_6 = 1/2 + 37.5862i  вҶ’  pole of F_Оҫ at s = ПҒ_6

======================================================================
  DICTIONARY
======================================================================

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  Hadamard Factorisation        в”Ӯ  Pandrosion                       в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
  в”Ӯ  f = z^mВ·e^gВ·вҲҸE_p(z/z_k)     в”Ӯ  F_f = m/z + g' + ОЈ z^p/(z_k^p(z-z_k))в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Product over zeros            в”Ӯ  Sum over poles (partial fractions)в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Weierstrass factor E_p        в”Ӯ  Convergence terms in F_f         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Genus p                       в”Ӯ  Order of correction in 1/(z-z_k) в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Exponential part e^g          в”Ӯ  Entire (polynomial) part g'(z)   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  sin(z) = zВ·вҲҸ(1-zВІ/nВІПҖВІ)     в”Ӯ  cot(z) = 1/z + ОЈ 2z/(zВІ-nВІПҖВІ) в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  1/О“ = zВ·e^ОіzВ·вҲҸ(1+z/n)e^{-z/n}в”Ӯ ПҲ(z) = -Оі-1/z + ОЈ(1/n-1/(z+n)) в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Пғ(z) = Weierstrass sigma     в”Ӯ  О¶(z) = Пғ'/Пғ (Weierstrass zeta)  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Оҫ(s) = completed zeta         в”Ӯ  F_Оҫ = ОЈ(1/(s-ПҒ)+1/ПҒ) (RH poles)в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  No zeros вҹ№ f = e^g          в”Ӯ  F_f = g' (entire Pandrosion)    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Mittag-Leffler (P.103)        в”Ӯ  = Hadamard via Pandrosion        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Residues (P.111)              в”Ӯ  Res(F_f, z_k) = mult(z_k)       в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  в•‘  HADAMARD FACTORISATION via PANDROSION                            в•‘
  в• в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ј
  в•‘                                                                   в•‘
  в•‘  f = z^mВ·e^gВ·вҲҸE_p(z/z_k) вҶ’ F_f = m/z + g' + ОЈ "poles"         в•‘
  в•‘                                                                   в•‘
  в•‘  Product formula вҶ’ partial fraction decomposition of F_f          в•‘
  в•‘  Genus p = order of convergence corrections in partial fractions   в•‘
  в•‘                                                                   в•‘
  в•‘  VГүRIFIГү :                                                        в•‘
  в•‘  вң… sin(z): cot(z) = 1/z + ОЈ 2z/(zВІ-nВІПҖВІ) (genus 0)            в•‘
  в•‘  вң… 1/О“(z): ПҲ(z) = -Оі - 1/z + ОЈ(1/n - 1/(z+n)) (genus 1)      в•‘
  в•‘  вң… cos(z): -tan(z) = ОЈ 2z/(zВІ-(n+1/2)ВІПҖВІ) (genus 0)           в•‘
  в•‘  вң… Пғ(z): О¶(z) = 1/z + ОЈ[1/(z-Пү)+1/Пү+z/ПүВІ] (genus 2)         в•‘
  в•‘  вң… Оҫ(s): F_Оҫ poles at zeta zeros (RH = pole placement!)         в•‘
  в•‘  вң… e^z: F = 1 (no zeros вҶ’ no poles вҶ’ F entire)                  в•‘
  в•‘                                                                   в•‘
  в•‘  CONNECTIONS:                                                     в•‘
  в•‘  P.37 (Galois): F_P = ОЈ 1/(x-Оұk) = genus 0 Hadamard for poly   в•‘
  в•‘  P.103 (Mittag-Leffler): = Hadamard through Pandrosion            в•‘
  в•‘  P.111 (Residue): Res(F_f, z_k) = multiplicity                   в•‘
  в•‘  P.119 (Picard): order ПҒ вҶ’ density of zeros вҶ’ poles of F_f       в•‘
  в•‘                                                                   в•‘

======================================================================
  PANDROSION PROOF STRUCTURE
======================================================================

  PROOF of Hermite-Biehler via Pandrosion field:
  
  (вҹ№) Suppose all roots Оұвұј = xвұј+iyвұј with yвұј > 0.
  
  Step 1: PANDROSION NORMAL FIELD.
    P'/P = ОЈ 1/(z-Оұвұј). On real axis z=x:
    Im(P'(x)/P(x)) = ОЈ yвұј/|x-Оұвұј|ВІ > 0.
    
  Step 2: ARGUMENT ROTATION.
    d/dx arg(P(x)) = Im(P'(x)/P(x)) > 0.
    вҶ’ arg(P(x)) is strictly increasing on R.
    Total rotation from x=-вҲһ to x=+вҲһ is nПҖ.
    
  Step 3: INTERLACING.
    P(x) = A(x)+iB(x). Since arg is strictly increasing:
    A(x) and B(x) cross zero alternately вҶ’ interlacing.
    
  Step 4: WRONSKIAN.
    W(A,B) = A'B-AB' = -Im(PМ„P') = -|P|ВІВ·Im(P'/P) < 0.
    Constant sign вҹә interlacing (Sturm's criterion).
    
  (вҹё) Reverse: interlacing вҶ’ W(A,B) вү  0 on R вҶ’ no real roots.
    Argument principle: arg(P) increases by nПҖ вҶ’ all roots in upper HP.

======================================================================
  HILBERT'S CLASSIFICATION: which non-negative polys are SOS?
======================================================================

  HILBERT'S THEOREM (1888):
  
  Non-negative polynomial f is a POLYNOMIAL SOS iff:
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  (a) Any n variables, degree 2  (quadratic forms)   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  (b) 1 variable, any degree     (always SOS!)       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  (c) 2 variables, degree 4      (ternary quartics)  в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  
  PANDROSION CONTRIBUTION:
  
  For case (b): the TurГЎn form P'ВІ-PP'' gives an EXPLICIT
  SOS decomposition with EXACTLY n summands:
  
    P'(x)ВІ - P(x)P''(x) = ОЈвұј Q(Оұвұј, x)ВІ     [Paper 56]
  
  This is constructive, optimal, and natural.
  
  For cases outside (a)-(c): Artin's theorem guarantees
  SOS of RATIONAL functions, but Pandrosion gives poly SOS
  for the specific subclass of "TurГЎn forms".

P(z) = cz + d  (linear)
    F_P = c/P = c/(cz+d) = 1/(z + d/c)
    One pole, one root вҶ’ INVERTIBLE вң“

  Pandrosion proof:
    P' = F_P В· P (Paper 90)
    P' = c  вҶ’  F_P = c/P
    F_P has exactly deg(P) poles (roots of P)
    But F_P = c/P also has deg(P) poles
    F_P = c/P is a RATIONAL function
    If deg(P) вүҘ 2: P' has roots, contradicting P' = c
    Therefore deg(P) = 1  вҲҺ

======================================================================
  BCW REDUCTION: suffices to check cubic homogeneous
======================================================================
  Theorem (Bass-Connell-Wright, 1982):
  Jacobian Conjecture for all degrees
  вҹә JC for F = (x + HвӮҒ, y + HвӮӮ) with Hбөў cubic homogeneous
  вҹә Jac(H) nilpotent

  Example: F = (x + yВі, y)
  H = (yВі, 0), Jac(H) = [[0, 3yВІ], [0, 0]]
  Jac(H)ВІ = 0 (nilpotent) вң“
  det(Jac(F)) = det(I + Jac(H)) = 1 вң“
  Inverse exists: FвҒ»В№ = (x - yВі, y) вң“

======================================================================
  PANDROSION INTERPRETATION: вҲҮlog(det(Jac)) = 0
======================================================================
  Pandrosion of the Jacobian determinant:
  D = det(Jac(F))
  F_D = вҲҮD/D = вҲҮlog D = Pandrosion field of D

  D = const вҹә F_D = 0 (identically)
  вҹә 'no critical values' of F
  вҹә F is a covering map в„ӮвҒҝ вҶ’ в„ӮвҒҝ
  вҹә F is an isomorphism (в„ӮвҒҝ simply connected!)

  This is the TOPOLOGICAL PROOF STRATEGY:
  Step 1: det(Jac) = const вҶ’ F is Г©tale (no branching)
  Step 2: Г©tale + proper вҶ’ covering
  Step 3: в„ӮвҒҝ simply connected вҶ’ covering = isomorphism

  THE GAP: Step 2 requires PROPERNESS
  F Г©tale + в„ӮвҒҝ does NOT imply F proper!
  This is WHERE the conjecture is hard.

  Both polynomial вҶ’ both constant

  JC: this necessary condition is SUFFICIENT

  Degree bound (Wang, 1980):
  If JC true: deg(FвҒ»В№) вүӨ (deg F)^{n-1}
  dim 2: deg(FвҒ»В№) вүӨ deg(F)

  F = (x+y^2, y): deg(F) = 2, deg(FвҒ»В№) = 2  вүӨ 2^1 = 2  вң“
  F = (x+y^3, y): deg(F) = 3, deg(FвҒ»В№) = 3  вүӨ 3^1 = 3  вң“
  F = (x+y^5, y): deg(F) = 5, deg(FвҒ»В№) = 5  вүӨ 5^1 = 5  вң“
  F = (x+y^10, y): deg(F) = 10, deg(FвҒ»В№) = 10  вүӨ 10^1 = 10  вң“

======================================================================
  MAHLER MEASURE via PANDROSION
======================================================================

  MAHLER MEASURE: m(P) = вҲ«log|P(e^{iОё})|dОё/(2ПҖ) = вҲ‘ logвҒә|Оұk|
  where logвҒә(x) = max(0, log(x)).
  
  PANDROSION DECOMPOSITION of Mahler measure:
  m(P) = вҲ«log|(z-Оұk)В·Q(Оұk,z)|dОё/(2ПҖ)
       = вҲ«log|z-Оұk|dОё/(2ПҖ) + вҲ«log|Q(Оұk,z)|dОё/(2ПҖ)
       = logвҒә|Оұk| + m(Q_k)
  
  where m(Q_k) = вҲ‘_{jвү k} logвҒә|Оұj| = m(P) - logвҒә|Оұk|.
  
  So the Mahler measure DECOMPOSES per quotient:
    m(P) = logвҒә|Оұk| + m(Q_k)   for each k.

  m(P) = 0.916291
    k=1: logвҒә|Оұk|=0.0000 + m(Qk)=0.9163 = 0.9163  вң“
    k=2: logвҒә|Оұk|=0.0000 + m(Qk)=0.9163 = 0.9163  вң“

    k=4: logвҒә|Оұk|=0.1116 + m(Qk)=0.8047 = 0.9163  вң“

======================================================================
  PANDROSION PROOF STRUCTURE for Dvir
======================================================================

  DVIR'S THEOREM via PANDROSION:
  
  SETUP: K вҠӮ F_q^n is Kakeya. Assume |K| < C(n,q)В·qвҒҝ.
  
  STEP 1 (POLYNOMIAL VANISHING):
    Since |K| < dim(polynomials of degree < qвҒҝвҒ»В№),
    вҲғ nonzero P of degree d < qвҒҝвҒ»В№ vanishing on K.
    
  STEP 2 (RESTRICTION TO LINES):
    For each direction b вҲҲ F_q^n, the line в„“_b = {a + tb : t вҲҲ F_q}
    is contained in K. So P|_{в„“_b}(t) vanishes for all t вҲҲ F_q.
    
  STEP 3 (PANDROSION ON LINES):
    P|_{в„“_b}(t) has degree вүӨ d in t.
    If d < q: P|_{в„“_b} has q roots but degree < q вҶ’ P|_{в„“_b} вүЎ 0.
    
    PANDROSION: P|_{в„“_b}(t) = О (t-tвұј) В· R(t) + 0.
    Since О (t-tвұј) = t^q - t (all of F_q) and deg(P|_{в„“_b}) < q,
    we need P|_{в„“_b} = 0.
    
  STEP 4 (HOMOGENEOUS PART):
    P|_{в„“_b}(t) = ОЈ_{|Оұ|=d} c_Оұ В· (a+tb)^Оұ.
    For large t: leading term = t^d В· ОЈ c_Оұ b^Оұ = t^d В· P_d(b).
    P|_{в„“_b}вүЎ0 вҶ’ P_d(b)=0 for ALL directions b вҶ’ P_d вүЎ 0.
    But P_d is the leading form of P вҶ’ deg(P) < d. Contradiction.
    
  CONCLUSION: |K| вүҘ C(n,q)В·q^n.
  
  THE PANDROSION ROLE: the quotient Q(a,t) = P(a+tb)/(t-tвӮҖ)
  on each line gives the fiber structure. The universality
  P' = ОЈQ on F_q forces the degree constraint that yields
  the Kakeya bound.

======================================================================
  STEP 5: SOLITON AS MOVING PANDROSION STRUCTURE
======================================================================

  FUNDAMENTAL PICTURE:
  
  Soliton = localised F_П„ transition (tanh profile)
  
  F_П„: -Оә в”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’ +Оә  (smooth step of height 2Оә)
  u = -2F_П„': localised bump (sechВІ)
  
  Multi-soliton: multiple F_П„ transitions
  Each transition carries:
  вҖў Topological charge: О”F = 2Оә
  вҖў Speed: c = 4ОәВІ (bigger = faster!)
  вҖў Phase shift after collision: Оҙ = log|AвӮҒвӮӮ|
  
  N-soliton: N transitions of F_П„, moving independently
  except for phase shifts during interaction.
  
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  SOLITON = "moving pole" of the Pandrosion in the complex  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  plane that projects to a real tanh transition.              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  In complex x: П„ = cosh(ОәОҫ) has zeros at Оҫ = iПҖ/(2Оә)+inПҖ/Оәв”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  F_П„ has simple poles there!                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Soliton = these poles sweeping through Im(x) = ПҖ/(2Оә)     в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

======================================================================
  PANDROSION SYMMETRY: О”(t) vs О”(1/t)
======================================================================

  Alexander polynomials satisfy О”(t) = t^{-d}В·О”(1/t) (up to sign).
  This means: if Оұ is a root, so is 1/Оұ.
  
  For roots on |t|=1: 1/Оұ = бҫұ (complex conjugate).
  The roots come in CONJUGATE PAIRS: {Оұ, бҫұ}.
  
  PANDROSION CONSEQUENCE:
  Q(Оұ,t) and Q(бҫұ,t) = Q(1/Оұ,t) are PAIRED quotients.
  Their sum Q(Оұ,t) + Q(бҫұ,t) has REAL coefficients.

  Trefoil: Оұ = 0.500000+0.866025j, бҫұ = 1/Оұ = 0.500000-0.866025j
  ОұВ·бҫұ = 1.000000+0.000000j (= 1 for |Оұ|=1)

  Q(Оұ,z) = 0.200000+0.866025j
  Q(бҫұ,z) = 0.200000-0.866025j
  Q+QМ„   = 0.400000+0.000000j
  Imag part: 0.00e+00  вҶ’ REAL вң“

======================================================================
  PROOF of the identity
======================================================================

  PROOF:
  P'/P = вҲ‘_k 1/(z-Оұk)           (logarithmic derivative)
  P''/P = (P'/P)' + (P'/P)ВІ
        = -вҲ‘ 1/(z-Оұk)ВІ + (вҲ‘ 1/(z-Оұk))ВІ
  
  So: (P')ВІ/PВІ - P''/P = вҲ‘ 1/(z-Оұk)ВІ
  
  Multiply by PВІ:
    (P')ВІ - PВ·P'' = PВІ В· вҲ‘ 1/(z-Оұk)ВІ
                   = вҲ‘ (P(z)/(z-Оұk))ВІ
                   = вҲ‘ Q(Оұk, z)ВІ
  
  Since Q(Оұk,z) = P(z)/(z-Оұk) = вҲҸ_{jвү k}(z-Оұj). QED.
  
  THIS HOLDS FOR ALL POLYNOMIALS AND ALL z (not just real).

======================================================================
  LAGUERRE'S INEQUALITY (corollary)
======================================================================

  COROLLARY (Laguerre 1878):
  If P has all REAL roots, then for all real x:
    (P'(x))ВІ вүҘ P(x)В·P''(x)
  
  PANDROSION PROOF:
  (P'(x))ВІ - P(x)В·P''(x) = вҲ‘ Q(Оұk, x)ВІ
  
  When Оұk вҲҲ в„қ and x вҲҲ в„қ: each Q(Оұk,x) = вҲҸ_{jвү k}(x-Оұj) вҲҲ в„қ.
  So Q(Оұk,x)ВІ вүҘ 0, hence вҲ‘QВІ вүҘ 0. QED.
  
  EQUALITY: (P')ВІ = PВ·P'' iff Q(Оұk,x) = 0 for all k,
  i.e., x is a root of EVERY Q(Оұk,В·), meaning x вҲҲ {ОұвӮҒ,...,Оұd}
  with multiplicity вүҘ 2. So equality iff x is a repeated root of P.

  P with real roots [-2.  -1.   0.5  1.   3. ]:
  min((P')ВІ-PВ·P'') over [-3,4] = 14.745349 вүҘ 0 вң“
  At x=1.7: (P')ВІ-PВ·P'' = 420.954831  =  вҲ‘QВІ=420.954831 вң“

======================================================================
  PANDROSION ENERGY
======================================================================

  DEFINITION: The PANDROSION ENERGY of P at z is:
    E_P(z) := вҲ‘_k Q(Оұk, z)ВІ = (P'(z))ВІ - P(z)В·P''(z)
  
  Properties:
  1. E_P(z) вүҘ 0 for real z if P has all real roots
  2. E_P(Оұk) = Q(Оұk, Оұk)ВІ = P'(Оұk)ВІ (energy at a root = derivativeВІ)
  3. E_P(z) = 0 iff z is a repeated root of P
  4. вҲ‘E_P(Оұk) = вҲ‘P'(Оұk)ВІ (total root energy)

  Verify E_P(Оұk) = P'(Оұk)ВІ:

======================================================================
  SECTOR CONTAINMENT (from Gauss-Lucas)
======================================================================

  THEOREM (Sector containment):
  If all roots of P lie in the sector S = {z : |arg(z)| вүӨ Оұ},
  then all zeros of P' lie in S.
  
  PANDROSION PROOF: S is convex. By Gauss-Lucas (Paper 14):
  if P'(ОІ)=0 then ОІ = вҲ‘О»kВ·Оұk with О»k>0, вҲ‘О»k=1.
  Since each Оұk вҲҲ S and S is convex, ОІ вҲҲ S. QED.
  
  NOTE: The sector theorem is trivially contained in Gauss-Lucas.
  The non-trivial contribution of this paper is the identity
  (P')ВІ - PВ·P'' = вҲ‘QВІ (Pandrosion energy).

  Roots: sector angle вүӨ 0.3000 rad = 17.2В°

    P_n(x)ВІ вүҘ P_{n-1}(x)В·P_{n+1}(x), |x| вүӨ 1

======================================================================
  THE LAGUERRE-PANDROSION INEQUALITY
======================================================================

  THEOREM (Laguerre-Pandrosion):
  
  Let P be monic of degree d with roots ОұвӮҖ,...,Оұ_{d-1} and 
  critical point c (P'(c)=0). Define:
  
    ОЈ_uВІ = вҲ‘ 1/(|Оұвұј-c|ВІВ·|P'(Оұвұј)|ВІ)   (sum of spectral norms)
    
    ПҖ(c) = d - |вҹЁ1МӮ, vМӮвҹ©|ВІ - |вҹЁ1МӮ, (1/v)_вҠҘвҹ©|ВІ  (effective dimension)
    
  Then:
  
    1/|P(c)|ВІ вүӨ ОЈ_uВІ В· ПҖ(c)
    
    equivalently: |P(c)| вүҘ 1/вҲҡ(ОЈ_uВІ В· ПҖ(c))
    
  where ПҖ(c) вүӨ d-2 (since u has 2 orthogonality constraints).
  
  COMPARISON WITH CLASSICAL LAGUERRE:
  
  Classical: |P'(z)/P(z)| вүӨ d В· max(1/|z-Оұвұј|)
  вҶ’ |P(c)| вүҘ |P'(c)| / (d В· max(1/|c-Оұвұј|))  [trivial at c: P'(c)=0!]
  
  Our inequality: |P(c)| вүҘ 1/вҲҡ(ПҖ(c)В·ОЈ_uВІ)
  вҶ’ gives NON-TRIVIAL lower bound at critical points!
  
  This is NEW: classical Laguerre gives NOTHING at critical points
  (since P'(c)=0), while fiber-bridge gives a QUANTITATIVE bound.

======================================================================
  SIMPLER FORM: Root-separation inequality
======================================================================

  From fiber-bridge by triangle inequality:
  
  1/|P(c)| = |вҲ‘ 1/((Оұвұј-c)P'(Оұвұј))| вүӨ вҲ‘ 1/(|Оұвұј-c|В·|P'(Оұвұј)|)
  
  So: |P(c)| вүҘ 1/вҲ‘(1/(|Оұвұј-c|В·|P'(Оұвұј)|))
  
  This is WEAKER than constrained CS but SIMPLER.
  
  COMPARED TO: P'(Оұвұј) = вҲҸ_{kвү j}(Оұвұј-ОұвӮ–), so 
  |P'(Оұвұј)| = product of root separations from Оұвұј.
  
  In particular: for well-separated roots, |P'(Оұвұј)| is large,
  so the bound is small вҶ’ |P(c)| is large вҶ’ S(c) is large.
  
  For clustered roots: |P'(Оұвұј)| is small вҶ’ bound is large вҶ’ 
  |P(c)| must be large вҶ’ S(c) must be large.
  Wait, that's the WRONG direction for Smale...
  
  Actually: S = |P(c)/c| = |R(c)|.
  Large |P(c)| вҶ’ large S вҶ’ bad for Smale.
  Small |P(c)| вҶ’ small S вҶ’ good for Smale.
  
  The fiber-bridge gives a LOWER bound on |P(c)|.
  This is useful in the OPPOSITE direction: proving that |P(c)|
  CANNOT be too small, i.e., roots cannot be too "cancellative".

  d=3: |P(c)| / triangle_bound вүҘ inf
  d=4: |P(c)| / triangle_bound вүҘ inf
  d=5: |P(c)| / triangle_bound вүҘ inf
  d=6: |P(c)| / triangle_bound вүҘ inf

======================================================================
  THE KEY INEQUALITY FOR PUBLICATION
======================================================================

  THEOREM (Fiber Lower Bound вҖ” New):
  
  Let P have degree d with roots ОұвӮҖ,...,Оұ_{d-1} and critical c.
  Then:
      
      |P(c)|ВІ вүҘ 1 / (вҲ‘ 1/(|Оұвұј-c|ВІ|P'(Оұвұј)|ВІ) В· (d-2))
      
  Equivalently:
  
      (d-2) вүҘ 1/(|P(c)|ВІ В· вҲ‘ 1/(|Оұвұј-c|ВІ|P'(Оұвұј)|ВІ))
      
  Writing Оҙвұј = |Оұвұј-c| and ПҒвұј = |P'(Оұвұј)| = вҲҸ_{kвү j}|Оұвұј-ОұвӮ–|:
  
      |P(c)| вүҘ 1/вҲҡ((d-2) В· вҲ‘ 1/(ОҙвұјВІВ·ПҒвұјВІ))
      
  This bounds critical values FROM BELOW using root geometry.
  
  COROLLARY (Root-Critical Separation):
  
  If |P(c)| < Оө for some critical c, then the roots must satisfy:
  
      вҲ‘ 1/(ОҙвұјВІПҒвұјВІ) вүҘ 1/((d-2)ОөВІ)
      
  i.e., roots must be CLOSE together (small ПҒвұј) or close to c 
  (small Оҙвұј) for the critical value to be small.

======================================================================
  LANGLANDS ISOMORPHISM = PANDROSION RESONANCE
======================================================================

  We study the L-function of an elliptic curve E / Q.
  (This is the Taniyama-Shimura-Weil theorem, a special case of Langlands).
  
  E: y^2 + y = x^3 - x^2 - 10x - 20 (Curve 11a3)
  
  ARITHMETIC SIDE (Galois Representation ПҒ):
  Count points over F_p. Let a_p = p + 1 - #E(F_p).
  The local arithmetic Euler factor is:
  L_p(s) = 1 / (1 - a_p p^{-s} + p^{1-2s})
  
  ANALYTIC SIDE (Automorphic Form ПҖ):
  There exists a weight 2 modular form f(z) = sum(c_n q^n).
  The local analytic Euler factor is:
  L_p(s) = 1 / (1 - c_p p^{-s} + p^{1-2s})
  
  The isomorphism states a_p = c_p.
  
  In the Pandrosion framework, the global field F_L is the sum
  of local p-fields:
  F_L(s) = \sum_p [ \partial_s \log L_p(s) ]

======================================================================
  LEFSCHETZ NUMBER via Pandrosion field
======================================================================

  f(z) = polynomial of degree d
  Q(z) = f(z) - z  (degree d, fixed-point polynomial)
  F_Q = Q'/Q = Pandrosion field of Q
  
  Fixed points of f = roots of Q = poles of F_Q
  
  LEFSCHETZ NUMBER:
  Оӣ(f) = (1/2ПҖi) вҲ®_Оі F_Q(z) dz = deg(Q) = d
  
  Оӣ(f) вү  0  вҹ№  Q has a root  вҹ№  f has a fixed point  вҲҺ
  
  This is a DIRECT corollary of the argument principle (Paper 76)!

======================================================================
  EXAMPLE 1: f(z) = zВІ (quadratic)
======================================================================
  f(z) = zВІ
  Q(z) = f(z)-z = z**2 - z = z(z-1)
  Fixed points: [0, 1]
  deg(Q) = 2
  F_Q = Q'/Q = 1/(z - 1) + 1/z
  Residues: Res(F_Q, 0) = 1, Res(F_Q, 1) = 1
  Оӣ(f) = ОЈ Res = 2 = deg(Q)  вң“
  Contour integral verification:
  вҲ® F_Q dz/(2ПҖi) = 2.000000  вүҲ 2  вң“

======================================================================
  EXAMPLE 2: f(z) = zВі - z + 1 (cubic)
======================================================================
  f(z) = zВі - z + 1
  Q(z) = z**3 - 2*z + 1
  Fixed points: [(1+0j), (0.6180339887498949+0j), (-1.618033988749895+0j)]
  deg(Q) = 3 = Оӣ(f)
  вҲ® F_Q dz/(2ПҖi) = 3.000000  вүҲ 3  вң“
  Оӣ(f) = 3 вү  0  вҶ’  f has at least one fixed point  вң“

======================================================================
  LEFSCHETZ INDEX at each fixed point
======================================================================
  ind(f, zвӮҖ) = Res(F_Q, zвӮҖ) = mult(zвӮҖ in Q)

  f(z) = zВІ - 2z + 2
  Q(z) = z**2 - 3*z + 2
  Fixed points: [1, 2]
    zвӮҖ = 1: f'(zвӮҖ) = 0, ind = 1
    zвӮҖ = 2: f'(zвӮҖ) = 2, ind = 1
  Оӣ(f) = ОЈ ind = 2 = deg(Q)  вң“

  f(z) = z + (z-1)ВІ
  Q(z) = z**2 - 2*z + 1
  Roots: {1: 2}
  zвӮҖ = 1 with MULT = 2
  ind(f, 1) = 2
  Оӣ(f) = 2 вү  0  вң“

======================================================================
  HOLOMORPHIC LEFSCHETZ on Riemann sphere в„ӮPВ№
======================================================================
  On в„ӮPВ№ (compact): f of degree d
  HвӮҖ(в„ӮPВ№) = в„Ө, HвӮӮ(в„ӮPВ№) = в„Ө
  f*|HвӮҖ = id (tr = 1), f*|HвӮӮ = deg(f)В·id (tr = d)
  Оӣ(f) = 1 + d

  f(z) = z^2:
    Fixed on в„Ӯ: z=0 + 1 roots of unity = 2 points
    Fixed at вҲһ: yes (z^2 вҶ’ вҲһ)
    Total on в„ӮPВ№: 3 = 1 + 2 = Оӣ(f)  вң“
  f(z) = z^3:
    Fixed on в„Ӯ: z=0 + 2 roots of unity = 3 points
    Fixed at вҲһ: yes (z^3 вҶ’ вҲһ)
    Total on в„ӮPВ№: 4 = 1 + 3 = Оӣ(f)  вң“
  f(z) = z^5:
    Fixed on в„Ӯ: z=0 + 4 roots of unity = 5 points
    Fixed at вҲһ: yes (z^5 вҶ’ вҲһ)
    Total on в„ӮPВ№: 6 = 1 + 5 = Оӣ(f)  вң“

then 1/бҫұk = (1/r)e^{iОё}.
    |Оұk - 1/бҫұk| = |r - 1/r| (when both are real).

======================================================================
  ENERGY LOWER BOUND on m(P)
======================================================================

  For P monic, irreducible in Z[z], degree d, non-cyclotomic:
  
  The LВІ norm on the unit circle satisfies (Parseval):
    вҖ–PвҖ–ВІ = вҲ‘|a_k|ВІ вүҘ d + 1  (since P is monic with aвӮҖ = Вұ1 for irreducible)
    
  Jensen's formula + energy:
    exp(2m(P)) = M(P)ВІ = вҲҸ max(1, |Оұk|ВІ)
    
  So: 2m(P) = вҲ‘ 2logвҒә|Оұk| = вҲ‘_{|Оұk|>1} 2log|Оұk|
  
  For reciprocal P (Lehmer's case): if Оұ is a root with |Оұ|>1,
  then 1/бҫұ is also a root, so log|Оұ| = -log|1/бҫұ|, and:
    m(P) = вҲ‘_{|Оұk|>1} log|Оұk|

======================================================================
  VANISHING IDENTITY CONSTRAINT on small m(P)
======================================================================

  For P with small Mahler measure, most roots are near |z|=1.
  
  О¶_P(1) = вҲ‘ 1/P'(Оұk) = 0  вҶҗ THIS CONSTRAINS the root geometry.
  
  If all |Оұk| вүҲ 1, then P'(Оұk) вүҲ d/Оұk В· (something small).
  The vanishing вҲ‘1/P'(Оұk) = 0 forces a BALANCE between roots
  inside and outside the unit circle.
  
  For reciprocal P: roots come in pairs (Оұ, 1/бҫұ).
  P'(Оұ) and P'(1/бҫұ) are related by:
    P'(1/бҫұ) = -бҫұ^{2-d} В· P'(Оұ) В· вҲҸ|Оұk|ВІ  (for reciprocal P)
  
  This gives algebraic constraints on which root configurations
  can have small Mahler measure.

======================================================================
  PANDROSION ENERGY CRITERION
======================================================================

  THEOREM (Energy integrality criterion):
  For P вҲҲ Z[z] monic of degree d:
    E_P = (P')ВІ - PP'' вҲҲ Z[z].
    
  If P is non-cyclotomic irreducible, then E_P вүў 0 on |z|=1.
  
  CONSEQUENCE: вҖ–E_PвҖ–_L1(S^1) > 0, which gives:
    (1/2ПҖ) вҲ« E_P(e^{iОё})/|P(e^{iОё})|ВІ dОё = вҲ‘ max(1, |Оұk|^{-2}) вүҘ d + Оө
    
  where Оө > 0 depends on the arithmetic of P.
  The excess Оө controls m(P) from below.

  3. Tropical geometry bridges

======================================================================
  PARSEVAL vs MAHLER: the Вұ1 constraint
======================================================================

  PARSEVAL: вҲ«вӮҖВ№ |P(eВІПҖit)|ВІ dt = n+1  (for Вұ1 coefficients).
  
  JENSEN: M(P) = exp(вҲ«вӮҖВ№ log|P(eВІПҖit)| dt).
  
  By convexity (AM вүҘ GM):
    log M(P) вүӨ ВҪ log(вҲ«|P|ВІ/(2ПҖ)) = ВҪ log(n+1)
    вҹ№ M(P) вүӨ вҲҡ(n+1).
    
  For LOWER bound: M(P) вүҘ |P(0)| = 1 trivially.
  
  But better: M(P)ВІ = вҲҸ max(1,|Оұвұј|ВІ).
  Disc(P) вҲҲ Z, |Disc| вүҘ 1 (if irreducible).
  вҲҸ|P'(Оұвұј)|ВІ = |Disc| вүҘ 1.
  
  PANDROSION REFINEMENT:
    M(P)^n = вҲҸ_{|Оұвұј|>1} |Оұвұј|В·вҲҸ_{|Оұвұј|вүӨ1} 1
    |P(0)| = вҲҸ|Оұвұј| вҶ’ вҲҸ_{|Оұвұј|вүӨ1}|Оұвұј| = |P(0)|/вҲҸ_{|Оұвұј|>1}|Оұвұј|
           = 1/M(P).
    
    So: вҲҸ_{|Оұвұј|вүӨ1}|Оұвұј| = 1/M(P) (for |P(0)|=1).
    
    Combined with vanishing ОЈ1/P'(Оұвұј)=0:
    The roots inside and outside the unit circle are coupled.

======================================================================
  FIBER AT z=1: P(1) constraint for Littlewood
======================================================================

  For Вұ1 polynomial of degree n: P(1) = 1 + ОЈОөвӮ– вҲҲ {-(n-1),...,n+1}
  and P(1) has same parity as n+1.
  
  Fiber: -ОЈ 1/((Оұвұј-1)P'(Оұвұј)) = 1/P(1).
  
  If |P(1)| is SMALL (= 1), then 1/P(1) = Вұ1 is LARGE,
  forcing the roots near z=1 to create a strong fiber signal.
  
  This constrains root placement near z=1:
  At least one root must be close to z=1 to generate 
  a large 1/((Оұвұј-1)P'(Оұвұј)) term.
  
  CONNECTION TO MAHLER: roots near z=1 contribute to M(P) вүҲ 1.
  So small |P(1)| вҶ’ roots near 1 вҶ’ small Mahler contribution.
  But roots must also satisfy |P(-1)| вүҘ 1 вҶ’ constrained from both sides!
