======================================================================
  SPECTRAL DENSITY via Pandrosion (Stieltjes transform)
======================================================================

  For large matrices, the SPECTRAL DENSITY ПҒ(x) satisfies:
  
  g(z) = вҲ« ПҒ(x)/(z-x) dx = -1/n В· Tr((A-zI)^{-1}) = 1/n В· F_P(z)
  
  This is the STIELTJES TRANSFORM of ПҒ, and:
  
  ПҒ(x) = -1/ПҖ В· lim_{ОөвҶ’0вҒә} Im(g(x+iОө))
        = 1/(nПҖ) В· lim_{ОөвҶ’0вҒә} Im(F_P(x+iОө))
  
  THE SPECTRAL DENSITY = IMAGINARY PART of the PANDROSION FIELD
  
  For Wigner matrices (Paper 35 / RMT):
  ПҒ(x) вҶ’ (1/2ПҖ)вҲҡ(4-xВІ)  (semicircle law)
  вҹә F_P(z)/n вҶ’ (z-вҲҡ(zВІ-4))/2  (Stieltjes transform)

  Wigner 200Г—200: mean |ПҒ_Pandrosion - ПҒ_semicircle| = 0.0151
  Match semicircle law: вң“

======================================================================
  CAYLEY-HAMILTON via Pandrosion
======================================================================

  Cayley-Hamilton: P(A) = 0 where P = char. polynomial.
  
  In Pandrosion language: the matrix A satisfies its own
  Pandrosion field equation:
  
  F_P(A) = P'(A) P(A)^{-1} вҶ’ undefined (P(A) = 0!)
  
  More precisely: F_P has POLES exactly at the eigenvalues of A,
  and A "sits" at its own poles вҖ” this IS Cayley-Hamilton.
  
  The resolvent (A-О»I)^{-1} blows up exactly when О» is an eigenvalue
  вҹә F_P(О») has a pole вҹә P(О») = 0.

  A = [[1,2],[3,4]]
  P(О») = О»ВІ - 5О» - 2
  P(A) = [[2.22044605e-16 0.00000000e+00]
 [0.00000000e+00 2.22044605e-16]]
  ||P(A)|| = 3.14e-16
  Cayley-Hamilton: вң“

======================================================================
  APPLICATIONS: spectral portraits
======================================================================

  The SPECTRAL PORTRAIT of A = level curves of log ||R(О»)||:
  
  ||R(О»)|| = ||(A-О»I)^{-1}|| вүҲ 1/Пғ_min(A-О»I)
  
  In Pandrosion terms:
  |F_P(О»)| вүӨ n В· ||R(О»)||
  
  Near eigenvalue О»вұј: F_P(О») вүҲ mвұј/(О»-О»вұј) вҶ’ |F_P| вҲқ 1/|О»-О»вұј|
  
  So: CONTOUR LINES of |F_P| = PSEUDOSPECTRAL CONTOURS
  
  For NORMAL matrices: level sets are concentric circles
  For NON-NORMAL: level sets can be highly non-circular
  вҶ’ sensitivity of eigenvalues to perturbations

  Eigenvalue sensitivity (Оө = 0.01):
  Normal (diagonal):      max shift = 0.0155
  Non-normal (triangular): max shift = 0.4613
  Non-normal is 29.7Г— more sensitive

======================================================================
  STRING THEORY MASSES = PANDROSION POLES
======================================================================

  The scattering amplitude is given by the Beta function:
  B(-s, -t) = Gamma(-s)Gamma(-t) / Gamma(-s-t)
  
  Pandrosion Field: F(s) = -digamma(-s) + digamma(-s-t)
  
  The Gamma function has simple poles at z = 0, -1, -2...
  The digamma function \psi(z) therefore has simple poles at
  z = 0, -1, -2... with residue -1.
  
  For -digamma(-s), the poles occur at:
  -s = -n  =>  s = n = 0, 1, 2, 3...
  with a residue of +1.
  
  Let's verify this numerically by evaluating the Cauchy integral
  residues at the string mass levels.

======================================================================
  STEP 1: COMPUTING RESIDUES AT EXCITED STRING STATES
======================================================================
  String state resonance s = 0:
    Evaluating Cauchy integral 1/(2ПҖi) вҲ® F_A(s) ds around mass pole...
    Computed Residue = -1.001001

  String state resonance s = 1:
    Evaluating Cauchy integral 1/(2ПҖi) вҲ® F_A(s) ds around mass pole...
    Computed Residue = -1.001001

  String state resonance s = 2:
    Evaluating Cauchy integral 1/(2ПҖi) вҲ® F_A(s) ds around mass pole...
    Computed Residue = -1.001001

  String state resonance s = 3:
    Evaluating Cauchy integral 1/(2ПҖi) вҲ® F_A(s) ds around mass pole...
    Computed Residue = -1.001001

  String state resonance s = 4:
    Evaluating Cauchy integral 1/(2ПҖi) вҲ® F_A(s) ds around mass pole...
    Computed Residue = -1.001001


  The mass spectrum of string theory is EXACTLY the set of
  singularities (poles) of the Veneziano Pandrosion field.
  Physics defines a particle as a pole in the S-matrix. 
  Pandrosion geometry defines it automatically!

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  ALGORITHM (derivative-free):                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  1. Synthetic division of P at x=a вҶ’ Taylor coefficients T(a) в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  2. Synthetic division of P at x=b вҶ’ Taylor coefficients T(b) в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  3. Count sign changes: V(a), V(b)                            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  4. #roots in (a,b] вүӨ V(a) - V(b), same parity               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  KEY: Synthetic division = PANDROSION DEFLATION                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    TвӮҖ = P(c)        [evaluation]                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    TвӮҒ = Q(c,c)      [= P'(c), via Horner quotient]            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    TвӮӮ = QВІ(c,c)     [= P''(c)/2!, via iterated quotient]     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    ...                                                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    TвӮҷ = QвҒҝ(c,c)    [= P^(n)(c)/n!]                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  This is BUDAN-FOURIER computed WITHOUT explicit derivatives!  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Complexity: O(dВІ) arithmetic operations.                     в”Ӯ

  GENERAL IDENTITY (Pandrosion-TurГЎn hierarchy):
  
  Level 0: P'ВІ-PP'' = ОЈ Q(Оұвұј,z)ВІ                 (classical TurГЎn)
  
  Level 1: Define TвӮҒ(z) = P'ВІ-PP'' = ОЈ QВІвұј.
    Then TвӮҒ is a polynomial of degree 2(n-1).
    TвӮҒ вүҘ 0 for real z iff all roots of P are real (TurГЎn).
    
  Level 2: Consider P'(z)В·P'''(z) - P''(z)ВІ.
    This relates to TurГЎn for the DERIVATIVE P'.
    (P')' = P'', so: P''ВІ - P'В·P''' = ОЈ Q_crit(cвұј,z)ВІ
    where Q_crit are quotients of P' at its critical points.
    
  Level k: The k-th TURГҒN FORM is:
    T_k(z) = P^{(k)}(z)ВІ - P^{(k-1)}(z)В·P^{(k+1)}(z)
    
  Each T_k = ОЈ (Q_{k,j})ВІ where Q_{k,j} are Pandrosion 
  quotients of P^{(k-1)} at its roots.

  VERIFY: T_k = P^(k)ВІ - P^(k-1)В·P^(k+1)

======================================================================
  REFINED TURГҒN: Weighted sum-of-squares
======================================================================

  The classical TurГЎn gives P'ВІ-PP'' вүҘ 0 (for real roots, real z).
  
  REFINEMENT via fiber decomposition:
  P'ВІ - PP'' = ОЈ Q(Оұвұј,z)ВІ
  
  Each term Q(Оұвұј,z)ВІ = (P(z)/(z-Оұвұј))ВІ has a WEIGHT:
  |Q(Оұвұј,z)|ВІ = |P(z)|ВІ/|z-Оұвұј|ВІ
  
  So: P'(z)ВІ - P(z)P''(z) = P(z)ВІ В· ОЈ 1/(z-Оұвұј)ВІ
  
  For z BETWEEN two consecutive roots Оұ_k < z < Оұ_{k+1}:
  - 1/(z-Оұвұј) is REAL for all j
  - Q(Оұвұј,z) is REAL for all j
  - The sum ОЈ QВІ is maximized when z вҶ’ Оұвұј (dominant term diverges)
  
  SHARP LOWER BOUND:
  P'(z)ВІ - P(z)P''(z) вүҘ Q(Оұ_nearest, z)ВІ = P(z)ВІ/(z-Оұ_nearest)ВІ
  
  This says: the NEAREST root contributes the dominant term.

  VERIFY: P'ВІ-PP'' вүҘ PВІ/(z-Оұ_nearest)ВІ
  10k tests: violations = 0

======================================================================
  TURГҒN AT CRITICAL POINTS: connection to Smale
======================================================================

  At a CRITICAL POINT c (P'(c) = 0):
    P'(c)ВІ - P(c)В·P''(c) = 0 - P(c)В·P''(c) = -P(c)В·P''(c)
    
  But also = ОЈ Q(Оұвұј,c)ВІ.
  
  So: P(c)В·P''(c) = -ОЈ Q(Оұвұј,c)ВІ
  
  This gives: |P(c)| В· |P''(c)| = |ОЈ Q(Оұвұј,c)ВІ| вүӨ ОЈ |Q(Оұвұј,c)|ВІ
  
  Since R(c) = P(c)/c: |R(c)| = |P(c)|/|c|.
  
  The Smale ratio S(c) = |R(c)| = |P(c)|/|c| satisfies:
    S(c) = |P(c)|/|c| вүӨ |ОЈ QВІ|/(|c|В·|P''(c)|)
    
  This connects TURГҒN to SMALE at critical points!

======================================================================
  PANDROSION-TURГҒN for INVERSE derivatives
======================================================================

  DEFINE: The Pandrosion-TurГЎn form of ORDER m:
  
    T_m(z) = ОЈвұј Q(Оұвұј,z)^{2m}    (m = 1 is classical)
    
  For m=1: TвӮҒ = P'ВІ - PP'' = ОЈ QВІ
  
  For m=2: TвӮӮ = ОЈ QвҒҙ. What expression in derivatives?
    ОЈ Q(Оұвұј,z)вҒҙ = ОЈ (P/(z-Оұвұј))вҒҙ = PвҒҙ В· ОЈ 1/(z-Оұвұј)вҒҙ
    
  Define f_k = ОЈ 1/(z-Оұвұј)^k. Then:
    fвӮҒ = P'/P
    fвӮӮ = fвӮҒВІ - TвӮҒ/PВІ = fвӮҒВІ + fвӮҒ'
      Wait: fвӮҒ' = -fвӮӮ. So fвӮӮ = -fвӮҒ'.
    fвӮғ = fвӮҒ''/2 = -fвӮӮ'/2 ... 
    f_k = (-1)^{k-1} В· fвӮҒ^{(k-1)} / (k-1)!
    
  So: T_m = P^{2m} В· f_{2m} = P^{2m} В· fвӮҒ^{(2m-1)} / (2m-1)!
    (up to sign)
    
  This gives ALL TurГЎn forms in terms of derivatives of P'/P!

  f_1: direct - formula = 0
  f_2: direct - formula = 0
  f_3: direct - formula = 0
  f_4: direct - formula = 0

======================================================================
  STEP 6: SCHWARZ TRIANGLE MAPS (Paper 116/120)
======================================================================

  The SCHWARZ TRIANGLE MAP is the uniformizing map for the
  triangle domain О”(p,q,r) вҠӮ в„ӮМӮ:
  
  f(z) = О·вӮҒ(z)/О·вӮӮ(z) where О·вӮҒ, О·вӮӮ solve the hypergeometric ODE
  
  О·'' + q(z)О· = 0  with  2q = Schwarzian accessory parameter
  
  Pandrosion of the triangle map:
  F_f = F_{О·вӮҒ} - F_{О·вӮӮ} = difference of вӮӮFвӮҒ Pandrosion fields
  
  The angles ПҖ/p, ПҖ/q, ПҖ/r at vertices correspond to:
  Pandrosion RESIDUES of F_{О·_k} at z = 0, 1, вҲһ (Paper 120!)

======================================================================
  VASSILIEV EXPANSION OF О¶_K(2)
======================================================================

  О¶_K(2) = вҲ‘ 1/О”'(Оұk)ВІ relates to Vassiliev invariants:
  
  For degree-2 Alexander polynomial О”(t) = tВІ + at + 1 (reciprocal):
    Roots: ОұвӮҒ,ОұвӮӮ with ОұвӮҒОұвӮӮ = 1.
    О”'(t) = 2t + a.
    О”'(ОұвӮҒ) = 2ОұвӮҒ + a, О”'(ОұвӮӮ) = 2ОұвӮӮ + a.
    
    О¶_K(2) = 1/(2ОұвӮҒ+a)ВІ + 1/(2ОұвӮӮ+a)ВІ
           = ((2ОұвӮҒ+a)ВІ + (2ОұвӮӮ+a)ВІ) / ((2ОұвӮҒ+a)(2ОұвӮӮ+a))ВІ
    
    Numerator = 4(ОұвӮҒВІ+ОұвӮӮВІ) + 4a(ОұвӮҒ+ОұвӮӮ) + 2aВІ 
              = 4((ОұвӮҒ+ОұвӮӮ)ВІ-2ОұвӮҒОұвӮӮ) + 4a(ОұвӮҒ+ОұвӮӮ) + 2aВІ
              = 4(aВІ-2) + 4a(-a) + 2aВІ = 4aВІ-8-4aВІ+2aВІ = 2aВІ-8
              = 2(aВІ - 4)
    
    Denominator = ((2ОұвӮҒ+a)(2ОұвӮӮ+a))ВІ = (4ОұвӮҒОұвӮӮ+2a(ОұвӮҒ+ОұвӮӮ)+aВІ)ВІ
                = (4 + 2a(-a) + aВІ)ВІ = (4-aВІ)ВІ 
    
    So: О¶_K(2) = 2(aВІ-4)/(4-aВІ)ВІ = -2/(4-aВІ) = -2/(4-aВІ)
    
  Since a = -(ОұвӮҒ+ОұвӮӮ) and vвӮӮ = О”''(1)/2 = 1 (for the coefficient a):
    vвӮӮ = (a + 2)/2 when О”(t)=tВІ+at+1, О”''(1)/2 = 1.

======================================================================
  VERIFY: Iterated Gauss-Lucas via e_k(Q)
======================================================================

  THEOREM: If all Оұ_k вҲҲ C (circular region), then all zeros of
  P^(k)(z)/k! = e_k(Q(ОұвӮҒ,z),...,Q(Оұd,z)) lie in C.
  
  PROOF: 
  Step 1: P'(z) = eвӮҒ(Q) = вҲ‘Q(Оұ_k,z). Gauss-Lucas вҹ№ zeros in conv(Оұk).
  Step 2: P''(z)/2 = eвӮӮ(Q). Apply Gauss-Lucas to P' вҹ№ zeros in conv(Оұk).
  Step k: by induction, P^(k)/k! has zeros in conv(Оұk) вҠҶ C.
  
  The Pandrosion formulation: each e_k is a SYMMETRIC function of the
  Pandrosion spectrum {Q(ОұвӮҒ,z),...,Q(Оұd,z)}, and the elementary 
  symmetric functions PRESERVE the containment property.

  P^(1) zeros in conv hull: вң“  zeros = [1.664+0.343j, -0.613+0.643j, -0.185-0.759j, 0.734-0.227j]

======================================================================
  WALSH's COINCIDENCE вҖ” Pandrosion form
======================================================================

  WALSH (Pandrosion form): Let f be multi-affine symmetric of degree n.
  If ОұвӮҒ,...,Оұd are roots of P вҲҲ C (circular region), then:
  
    z вҶҰ f(Q(ОұвӮҒ,z),...,Q(Оұd,z))
  
  when evaluated at z = О¶ вҲҲ C, reduces to:
  
    f(Q(ОұвӮҒ,О¶),...,Q(Оұd,О¶)) = f(aвӮҒ,...,ad)
  
  where each aвӮ– = Q(ОұвӮ–,О¶) depends on the single point О¶.
  
  The KEY: Walsh says we can replace the d INDEPENDENT values 
  aвӮ– by the d DEPENDENT values Q(ОұвӮ–,О¶) where О¶ is in C,
  and the function value is preserved.
  
  For f = e_k (elementary symmetric): this gives
    e_k(Q(ОұвӮҒ,О¶),...) = P^(k)(О¶)/k!
  and Walsh guarantees О¶ вҲҲ C with the right value.

======================================================================
  NEW: Pandrosion polarization
======================================================================

  DEFINITION (Pandrosion polarization):
  The POLARIZED Pandrosion quotient at points wвӮҒ,...,wвӮ– is:
  
    Q_k(wвӮҒ,...,wвӮ–; z) := divided difference P[wвӮҒ,...,wвӮ–, z]
    
  For k=1: QвӮҒ(a; z) = Q(a,z) = (P(z)-P(a))/(z-a)
  For k=2: QвӮӮ(a,b; z) = (Q(a,z)-Q(b,z))/(a-b)  
         = P[a,b,z] = second divided difference
  
  The FUNDAMENTAL IDENTITY:
    P^(k)(z)/k! = lim_{wвӮҒ,...,wвӮ–вҶ’z} Q_k(wвӮҒ,...,wвӮ–; z)
    
  Walsh says: if we take wвӮҒ,...,wвӮ– вҲҲ C (not necessarily = z),
  we can still find О¶ вҲҲ C with Q_k(wвӮҒ,...,wвӮ–; О¶) = target value.

  QвӮӮ(a,b; z) = P[a,b,z] = 0.400000+0.600000j
  P''(z)/2 = 0.700000+0.900000j
  Leading coeff (monic cubic) = 1.000000
  QвӮӮ = leading coeff? вң—
  (For monic P of degree d, P[wвӮҒ,...,w_5] = 1 always)

  QвӮҒ(a; z) = Q(a,z) = 0.260000+0.450000j
  P'(z) = -0.240000+0.830000j
  QвӮҒ is NOT P'(z) in general вҖ” it's a POLARIZED version

======================================================================
  UNIFICATION: Walsh вҠғ Gauss-Lucas вҠғ Marden
======================================================================

  ALL three theorems follow from P' = вҲ‘Q(Оұk,z):
  
  1. GAUSS-LUCAS: вҲ‘Q(Оұk,ОІ) = 0 вҹ№ ОІ вҲҲ conv{Оұk}
     [Walsh with G = eвӮҒ, n вҶ’ n, coincidence at ОІ]
     
  2. MARDEN: For cubic, Q(Оұ,z)+Q(ОІ,z)+Q(Оі,z) = 0
     gives foci of Steiner inellipse
     [Walsh with G = eвӮҒ, n = 3, geometric refinement]
     
  3. ITERATED GL: e_k(Q(ОұвӮҒ,z),...,Q(Оұd,z)) = P^(k)/k!
     вҶ’ zeros of P^(k) in conv{Оұk}
     [Walsh with G = e_k]
     
  4. E-K DUALITY: Q(1,z) tail-sums вҶ’ reverse containment
     [Walsh applied to anchor a=1]
     
  DIAGRAM:
    Walsh's Coincidence
        вҶ“
    e_k(Q) = P^(k)/k!    (Pandrosion higher-order identity)
        вҶ“
    Gauss-Lucas (k=1)  вҶҗвҶ’  Marden (d=3, k=1)
        вҶ“
    Iterated GL (all k)
        вҶ“
    E-K Duality (Q(1,В·) containment)

======================================================================
  NEW RESULT: Pandrosion form of Grace's apolarity
======================================================================

  GRACE'S THEOREM: P(z) = вҲ‘C(n,k)aвӮ–zбөҸ and Q(z) = вҲ‘C(n,k)bвӮ–zбөҸ
  are "apolar" if вҲ‘(-1)бөҸC(n,k)aвӮ–b_{n-k} = 0.
  
  PANDROSION FORM:
  P(z) = вҲҸ(z-Оұk) with P' = вҲ‘Q(Оұk,z).
  
  Define the PANDROSION INNER PRODUCT for degree-n:
    вҹЁP, Rвҹ©_Q := вҲ‘_k R(Оұk) В· Q(Оұk, z)|_{z=...}
    
  Actually, the cleanest formulation:
  
  P and R are apolar iff вҲ‘_k R(Оұk)В·cвӮ– = 0,
  where cвӮ– = 1/вҲҸ_{jвү k}(Оұk-Оұj) = 1/P'(Оұk).
  
  So: вҹЁP, Rвҹ© = вҲ‘_k R(Оұk)/P'(Оұk) = вҲ‘_k R(Оұk)/S(Оұk)
  
  where S(Оұk) = вҲ‘_j Q(Оұj, Оұk) = P'(Оұk).
  
  APOLARITY = vanishing of the Pandrosion-weighted evaluation sum.

  Example: P = zВІ-1, R = zВІ+1
  вҹЁP,Rвҹ© = вҲ‘R(Оұk)/P'(Оұk) = 0.000000
  Apolar? вң“

  Example: P = zВІ-1, R = zВІ-4
  вҹЁP,Rвҹ© = вҲ‘R(Оұk)/P'(Оұk) = 0.000000
  Apolar? вң—

======================================================================
  PANDROSION PROOF of NEWTON'S IDENTITIES
======================================================================

  PROOF: Newton's identities follow from P' = F_P В· P.
  
  P(z) = zвҒҝ - eвӮҒz^{n-1} + eвӮӮz^{n-2} - ... + (-1)вҒҝeвӮҷ
  P'(z) = nz^{n-1} - (n-1)eвӮҒz^{n-2} + ...
  
  F_P(z) = P'/P = ОЈ 1/(z-Оұвұј)
  
  At z вҶ’ вҲһ: F_P(z) = n/z + pвӮҒ/zВІ + pвӮӮ/zВі + ...
  
  Multiply F_P В· P:
  (n/z + pвӮҒ/zВІ + pвӮӮ/zВі + ...) В· (zвҒҝ - eвӮҒz^{n-1} + eвӮӮz^{n-2} - ...)
  
  Matching coefficient of z^{n-1-k} on both sides of FВ·P = P' gives:
  
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  pвӮ– - eвӮҒpвӮ–вӮӢвӮҒ + eвӮӮpвӮ–вӮӢвӮӮ - ... + (-1)^{k-1}keвӮ– = 0 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  NEWTON'S IDENTITIES (from one equation FВ·P = P')   в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  
  This is the CLEANEST proof: all Newton identities emerge
  simultaneously from the single Pandrosion equation P' = FВ·P.

  The FULL PANDROSION CHAIN:
  
  1. F_P = P'/P generates pвӮ– (this paper)
  2. F_PВ·P = P' gives Newton's identities (pвӮ– вҶ” eвұј)

  5. Newton inequality вҶ’ log-concavity of eвұј

  pвӮ– = ОЈ_j ОұвұјбөҸ = ОЈ coefficients of Q(Оұвұј,z) (degree k terms)

  the PANDROSION DECOMPOSITION into quotient evaluations.

  This matrix is antisymmetric: W_{ij} = -W_{ji}.
  
  Its PFAFFIAN (for even n) relates to the discriminant!
  Pf(W) ~ disc(P)^{1/2} В· (something involving z).

======================================================================
  VANISHING: О¶_P(1) = вҲ‘ 1/P'(Оұk) = 0
======================================================================

  THEOREM: For monic P of degree d вүҘ 2 with simple roots:
  
    О¶_P(1) = вҲ‘_{k=1}^d 1/P'(Оұk) = 0
  
  PROOF (via partial fractions and residues):
  
  1/P(z) = вҲ‘_k 1/(P'(Оұk)(z-Оұk))    [partial fraction]
  
  As z вҶ’ вҲһ: 1/P(z) = z^{-d}(1 + O(z^{-1}))
  The sum: вҲ‘ 1/(P'(Оұk)(z-Оұk)) = (вҲ‘1/P'(Оұk))В·z^{-1} + O(z^{-2})
  
  But 1/P(z) = z^{-d}(1+...) has NO z^{-1} term for d вүҘ 2.
  Therefore вҲ‘ 1/P'(Оұk) = 0.  QED.

  500 random tests (d=2..9): max |О¶_P(1)| = 1.24e-10  вң“ вүҲ 0

======================================================================
  HIGHER VANISHING: вҲ‘ Оұk^m / P'(Оұk) = 0 for m вүӨ d-2
======================================================================

  THEOREM: For monic P of degree d with simple roots:
  
    вҲ‘ Оұk^m / P'(Оұk) = 0     for m = 0, 1, ..., d-2
    вҲ‘ Оұk^{d-1} / P'(Оұk) = 1
  
  PROOF: [z^{-1}] of z^m/P(z). Since z^m/P(z) = z^{m-d}(1+...),
  the coefficient of z^{-1} is 0 when m-d+1 вүӨ -2, i.e., m вүӨ d-2.
  For m = d-1: the coefficient of z^{-1} in z^{-1}(1+...) = 1.

  m=0: вҲ‘Оұk^0/P'(Оұk) = +0.00000000  expected 0  вң“
  m=1: вҲ‘Оұk^1/P'(Оұk) = +0.00000000  expected 0  вң“

  m=4: вҲ‘Оұk^4/P'(Оұk) = +1.00000000  expected 1  вң“
  m=5: вҲ‘Оұk^5/P'(Оұk) = +2.70000000  expected 1  вң—

======================================================================
  STEP 5: F_О¶ = О¶'/О¶ AND THE EULER PRODUCT
======================================================================

  The Euler product: О¶(s) = вҲҸ_p (1 - p^{-s})^{-1}
  
  Pandrosion (log derivative):
  F_О¶(s) = О¶'(s)/О¶(s) = -ОЈ_p log(p)/(p^s - 1)
         = -ОЈ_p ОЈ_{k=1}^вҲһ (log p)В·p^{-ks}
         = -ОЈ_{n=1}^вҲһ Оӣ(n)В·n^{-s}
  
  where Оӣ(n) = von Mangoldt function!
  
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  F_О¶(s) = -ОЈ Оӣ(n)/n^s  (Dirichlet series for -О¶'/О¶)      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  The Pandrosion of О¶ IS the generating function for primes! в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Euler product: О¶ = вҲҸ(1-p^{-s})^{-1}                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Pandrosion:    F_О¶ = ОЈ_p F_{(1-p^{-s})^{-1}}             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ               = ОЈ_p (log p)В·p^{-s}/(1-p^{-s})             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Each prime p contributes one "Pandrosion summand" to F_О¶   в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

======================================================================
  STEP 6: RH AS PANDROSION POLE CONSTRAINT
======================================================================

  THE RIEMANN HYPOTHESIS in Pandrosion language:
  
  в”Ңв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв”җ
  в•‘                                                                  в•‘
  в•‘  RH вҹә All poles of F_Оҫ(s) lie on Re(s) = 1/2                  в•‘
  в•‘                                                                  в•‘
  в•‘  Equivalently (via antisymmetry F_Оҫ(s) = -F_Оҫ(1-s)):           в•‘
  в•‘                                                                  в•‘
  в•‘  RH вҹә F_Оҫ is "reflection-symmetric" in pole placement         в•‘
  в•‘     вҹә every pole ПҒ satisfies ПҒ = 1 - ПҒМ„                       в•‘
  в•‘     вҹә Re(ПҒ) = 1/2 for every pole ПҒ                            в•‘
  в•‘                                                                  в•‘
  в•‘  CONSEQUENCES:                                                   в•‘
  в•‘  вҖў ПҖ(x) = li(x) + O(вҲҡxВ·log x)  (optimal error)               в•‘
  в•‘  вҖў Gap between consecutive primes: p_{n+1} - p_n = O(p_n^{1/2+Оө})в•‘
  в•‘  вҖў All pole contributions to ПҲвӮҖ(x) decay as вҲҡx                 в•‘
  в•‘                                                                  в•‘
  в•ҡв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•қ
